programme.title: Am Dro!
programme.desc: World-famous conductor Alwyn Humphreys this time guides the others along Beaumaris to Penmon coastal path. Actress Sue Roderick prepares for a walk along the iconic Lon Goed (Road of the Trees) of her childhood. Emma Jenkins, aka Miss Universe, uses the stunning coastal path at Pembrey Country Park as her catwalk, while weatherman and drummer Owain Wyn Evans searches for a historical site that holds special memories for him on the Brecon Beacons

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Roger Johnson and Nina Warhurst present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the Boxing Day Premier League matches, including Arsenal v Chelsea at Emirates Stadium and Leicester City v Manchester United at King Power Stadium. The Blues defeated their London rivals 2-1 in last season's corresponding fixture, while United were looking to emulate their 2-0 victory at Leicester last term. Plus, Aston Villa v Crystal Palace, Fulham v Southampton, Manchester City v Newcastle United and Sheffield United v Everton

programme.title: Sunday Worship
programme.desc: On the first Sunday of Christmas, the Rev Canon Mark Preece, Canon Precentor, leads the morning worship from Llandaff Cathedral in south Wales. With hymns and carols from the Songs of Praise archives and a new recording from the Cardiff-based choir Côrdydd

programme.title: Mimi and the Mountain Dragon
programme.desc: Animated adaptation of Michael Morpurgo's children's book about a brave but shy girl from a village that lives in constant fear of a terrible mountain dragon. On a snowy winter's night, she finds a baby dragon asleep in her family's woodshed, and sets out on a terrible journey through a blizzard to return the infant to its dreaded mother. Featuring an introduction by the author and a score by Oscar-winning composer Rachel Portman, performed by the BBC Philharmonic

programme.title: The Penguins of Madagascar
programme.desc: Skipper, Kowalski, Private and Rico embark on an adventure on Christmas Eve. Animated film spin-off of the popular films, featuring the voices of John DiMaggio and Tom McGrath

programme.title: Shaun the Sheep Movie (2015)
programme.desc: The lovable lamb's plans for a fun day off turn into something far more exciting when he's forced to take a trip to the big city to save the farmer, who has lost his memory. Can Shaun and his friends return home safely following their adventure? Animation, featuring the voices of Justin Fletcher, John Sparkes and Omid Djalili

programme.title: Wallace & Gromit in The Curse of the Were-Rabbit (2005)
programme.desc: Inventor Wallace and his canine sidekick Gromit have set up a pest-control service, and are called in to stop rabbits overrunning a garden show. However, the pair are overwhelmed when a destructive giant bunny arrives on the scene, while an interfering aristocrat contrives to make matters worse. Nick Park's Oscar-winning animated adventure, with the voices of Peter Sallis, Ralph Fiennes and Helena Bonham Carter

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Choir director and vocal coach Mark De-Lisser joins Soul singer Mica Paris as she shares her journey of faith, and speaks about the strength of their family unit. Featuring favourite hymns and three Gospel performances from Mica herself

programme.title: Monsters University (2013)
programme.desc: Student creatures Mike Wazowski and James P Sullivan, aka Sulley, attend university to learn how to be scary, but their rivalry gets them both kicked off the course. The monstrous duo must learn how to work together if they are to realise their dreams of terrifying children. Winning the Scare Games will make the difference between Mike staying at the campus or having to leave. Pixar's animated comedy prequel to Monsters Inc, with the voices of John Goodman, Billy Crystal, Steve Buscemi and Helen Mirren

programme.title: Book of Dragons (2011)
programme.desc: Animated short based on the 2010 fantasy film How to Train Your Dragon. Hiccup, Astrid, Fishlegs, Toothless and Gobber uncover the legend behind the Book of Dragons and reveal insider training secrets regarding some new, never-before-seen mythical creatures. With the voices of Jay Baruchel and Craig Ferguson

programme.title: The Sound of Music (1965)
programme.desc: A cheerful novice nun is employed as governess to the seven children of an austere widower. As she brings light, laughter and a love of all things harmonious to a family previously beset by grief and strict discipline she also falls in love with her employer. But before long, the looming threat of the Second World War jeopardises their happy household. Oscar-winning Rodgers and Hammerstein musical, starring Julie Andrews, Christopher Plummer and Eleanor Parker

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison meets Simon King in Bristol to get a crash course in how to photograph wildlife. They attempt to capture on camera birds in flight over the Avon Gorge, inquisitive city squirrels, and a herd of deer who live semi-wild in the nearby Ashton Court Estate. Plus, a look through Countryfile's archive for some of Ellie's wildlife highlights

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce revisits some of the show's most exciting finds, and discovers what happened when the cameras stopped rolling. She reveals the outcome of the auction for a special guitar belonging to the Beatles and the mystery of the missing blueprint for the Royal Albert Hall, before going behind the scenes of Pinewood Film and TV Studios to learn about its wartime history, its famous costumes, and a giant golden gong

programme.title: Black Narcissus
programme.desc: Sister Clodagh and her group of Anglo-Catholic nuns travel from Darjeeling on a mission to the Palace of Mopu in the Himalayas. The order starts to repair the palace and opens a school. However, the unsettling atmosphere of their surroundings reawakens disturbing memories in Clodagh. Adaptation of Rumer Godden's novel, starring Gemma Arterton

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the day's Premier League action, including Liverpool v West Bromwich Albion at Anfield and Wolverhampton Wanderers v Tottenham Hotspur at Molineux. Although they have not been as dominant as last term, the Reds are chasing a second successive league championship and will have expected to prevail against the Baggies, while Spurs are also chasing the title and faced a real test of their credentials against Wolves. Plus, Leeds United v Burnley, West Ham United v Brighton & Hove Albion and a round-up of yesterday's goals

programme.title: Spider-Man: Homecoming (2017)
programme.desc: Peter Parker balances life as an ordinary high-school student with his superhero alter-ego Spider-Man, while waiting in vain to join his mentor Iron Man on a world-saving mission. Determined to prove he is ready for bigger challenges, he investigates a spree of crimes committed with advanced weapons - leading him into a battle with a ruthless winged enemy on the hunt for priceless technology. Superhero adventure, starring Tom Holland, Michael Keaton and Robert Downey Jr

programme.title: Famalam
programme.desc: Highlights from the comedy sketch show, including an overprotective boyfriend meeting his match, and Scribbler P giving an important lesson on military history. Plus, the competitive aunties cause havoc, Midsomer receives an injection of diversity as Detective Mountree solves a whodunit in a stately home and Fantastic Egusi Films give their inimitable rendition of a Hollywood blockbuster. Starring Vivienne Acheampong, Gbemisola Ikumelo, Samson Kayo, John Macmillan, Tom Moutchi and Akemnji Ndifornyen

programme.title: West Is West (2010)
programme.desc: A traditionally minded Muslim father living in 1970s Salford decides the solution to his troubled youngest son's problems is to reconnect with his roots. He takes the reluctant teenager on a trip to Pakistan, only to have an awkward encounter with his first wife. Comedy sequel to East Is East, with Om Puri, Aqib Khan and Linda Bassett

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Jon Kay and Nina Warhurst present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Planes (2013)
programme.desc: A crop duster longs to be a racing plane, but is hamstrung by a fear of heights. With the aid of a veteran flyer, he learns to conquer his phobia and compete against some of the world's greatest flyers in a round-the-world race. Animated adventure spin-off from Pixar's Cars, with the voices of Dane Cook, Teri Hatcher and Stacy Keach

programme.title: Paddington 2 (2017)
programme.desc: The lovable little bear has settled into his new life with the Brown family in London, becoming a much-loved member of the local community - until his search for a birthday present for his aunt leads to him being framed as a thief. While the Browns try to clear Paddington's name, he tries to make himself at home in prison, winning over the other convicts and getting involved in an escape attempt. Comedy adventure, starring Hugh Bonneville, Hugh Grant and Sally Hawkins, with Ben Whishaw providing the voice of Paddington

programme.title: Revolting Rhymes
programme.desc: The first of two collections of animations based on poems from Roald Dahl's children's book, featuring the author's twists on Snow White & the Seven Dwarves, Little Red Riding Hood and The Three Little Pigs. Best friends Snow and Red take on a villainous queen with a magic mirror, before Red faces another challenge when two hungry wolves are on the prowl - one disguised as her own grandmother, while the other has his eye on a trio of helpless pigs. With the voices of Tamsin Greig, Dominic West, Rob Brydon, Bertie Carvel, Gemma Chan and Rose Leslie

programme.title: Revolting Rhymes
programme.desc: Conclusion. Two more animations based on tales from Roald Dahl's children's book, which puts the author's comic spin on the stories of Cinderella and Jack and the Beanstalk. Jack is madly in love with his next door neighbour Cindy - but after attending a ball, she only has eyes for a vain prince. He finds himself distracted from the pursuit of true love when a giant beanstalk starts growing at the bottom of his garden. The star-studded voice cast includes David Walliams and Rob Brydon as the ugly sisters

programme.title: Inside Out (2015)
programme.desc: Inside 11-year-old Riley's head lives a team of five that controls her emotions. Led by the eternally upbeat Joy, they have always ensured she lived a happy life - but when Riley's parents move to a new city, she is plunged into depression and the emotions face a crisis that threatens their entire world. Pixar animated fantasy, with the voices of Amy Poehler and Phyllis Smith

programme.title: How to Train Your Dragon: Legend of the Boneknapper Dragon
programme.desc: Animated short film featuring the characters from How to Train Your Dragon. Gobber's house is burned down, and he becomes convinced a legendary monster is responsible. He sets off on a quest to slay the beast, but none of his friends believe it even exists. With the voices of Craig Ferguson, Jay Baruchel and Gerard Butler

programme.title: The Jungle Book (2016)
programme.desc: A boy is raised by animals in the jungles of India, but his carefree life is threatened by the return of a fierce tiger with a grudge against humans. The youngster faces a difficult choice between remaining with his friends or returning to his own people. Live action remake of Disney's animated adventure based on Rudyard Kipling's stories, starring Neel Sethi, with the voices of Bill Murray, Ben Kingsley and Idris Elba

programme.title: Wallace & Gromit: A Matter of Loaf and Death
programme.desc: The duo open their own bakery, Top Bun, and business is soon booming - not least because a 'cereal killer' is targeting all the rival bakers in town. Even better, Wallace has caught the eye of local beauty Piella Bakewell. But Gromit is more concerned that his master might become the killer's next victim. With the voices of Peter Sallis and Sally Lindsay

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present as famous faces take part in a festive edition of the general knowledge quiz, in which they try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. Taking part are Slade guitarist Dave Hill, Mud's Rob Davis, actress Sally Lindsay, poet and broadcaster Roger McGough, and singers Sonia, Jamelia, Keren Woodward and Sara Dallin

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: Former winners Miles Jupp, Zoe Lyons, Neil Hannon and Lucy Porter are put to the test by John Humphrys in a special champion of champions edition of the quiz, answering questions on the specialist subjects of David Gower, Jacques Cousteau, Merchant Ivory's EM Forster film adaptations and Victoria Wood

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games Night
programme.desc: Sarah Hadland, Alex Horne, Craig Revel Horwood and Charlene White compete in a festive edition of the general knowledge quiz, taking on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host. A winner will be declared following a quick-fire round at the end of the show, and the victor takes home a prize and the House of Games Christmas trophy

programme.title: Dynasties
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates the story of young meerkat queen Maghogho on her quest to establish a clan in one of the harshest places on earth. The territory on the Makgadikgadi salt pans in Botswana is at the limit of what meerkats can endure and yet it is here that she must raise her pups in the face of rivals, predators and immense dust storms

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Max puts pressure on Linda as things between them start to become very real. Martin tells Ruby he wishes she could bury the hatchet with Stacey, while Shirley is disappointed that Mick still refuses to engage with her. Denise opens up about her fears that Lucas will hurt her - so Kim comes up with a plan

programme.title: Black Narcissus
programme.desc: Sister Ruth's health worsens as winter sets in and the ghost of Srimati plagues her as Angu Ayah tells her more of the princess's tragic fate. Dilip Rai's arrival in search of education presents Sister Clodagh with a dilemma and she is forced to turn to Mr Dean for help, and as she struggles to keep control of the convent, Sister Philippa announces her desire to leave. Forbidden feelings between Clodagh, Sister Ruth and Mr Dean come to a head at Christmas, interrupted by the dramatic arrival of Sister Adela and Fr Roberts

programme.title: Minority Report (2002)
programme.desc: Cop John Anderton is forced to go on the run when a supposedly infallible trio of psychics foretell he is about to kill a man he has never heard of. Convinced their prediction is false, he kidnaps one of the so-called precogs to prove his innocence - and stumbles across a complex conspiracy. Steven Spielberg's futuristic thriller, starring Tom Cruise, Colin Farrell, Samantha Morton and Max von Sydow

programme.title: Man Like Mobeen
programme.desc: While trying to do a good deed by helping out at the local food bank, Mobeen is also under strict orders from Uncle Khan to launder money - and juggling the two things could leave him with a deadly debt that's impossible to pay

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: A compilation of the popular news looking back at the big news of 2019, a year that was largely dominated by Brexit. Eventually a General Election was held - and the rest is history. Mind you, there was some joy courtesy of the England cricket team, with their memorable victory over New Zealand in the World Cup, even if it was by the slightest of margins. Team captains Paul Merton and Ian Hislop are joined by a variety of guest hosts and panellists from the worlds of comedy, journalism and current affairs in highlights of the year's shows

programme.title: Mindhorn (2016)
programme.desc: The star of a 1980s sci-fi cop show sets out to make it in Hollywood, but ends up with his life and career in ruins. Years later, he gets the chance to be a real hero when the police need his help. A dangerous fugitive is on the loose, who believes the character the actor played - a detective with a bionic eye that can detect lies - is real. Comedy, starring Julian Barratt, Essie Davis and Steve Coogan

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Jon Kay and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: The NeverEnding Story (1984)
programme.desc: A lonely boy, neglected by his father and bullied at school, finds comfort in a mysterious book about a young hero's quest to save his world from destruction. However, as he reads the tale, he begins to become part of the story. Fantasy adventure based on Michael Edne's novel, starring Barret Oliver, Noah Hathaway, Gerald McRaney, Moses Gunn and Patricia Hayes

programme.title: The Croods (2013)
programme.desc: Prehistoric family the Croods find themselves with nowhere to live when their cave is destroyed. They embark on a dangerous trek through an unstable landscape overrun with all manner of dangerous predators in search of a new home, but their domestic squabbles prove as much of an obstacle as the wildlife. Animated comedy, with the voices of Nicolas Cage and Ryan Reynolds

programme.title: Mr Stink (2012)
programme.desc: Family comedy based on the children's novel by David Walliams. Twelve-year-old Chloe leads a lonely existence - her mother is too busy with her political ambitions to take any interest in her daughter, her hen-pecked dad is hiding a secret of his own and she cannot hope to compete with her 'perfect' younger sister - to say nothing of the nasty girls who make her school life a misery. So when she meets a tramp in need of somewhere to stay, she invites him to hide in her family's garden shed - but it soon turns out there is more to her smelly guest than meets the eye. Downton Abbey's Hugh Bonneville stars, with Sheridan Smith, Johnny Vegas and Isabella Blake-Thomas, and an appearance from Britain's Got Talent winner Pudsey the dog

programme.title: The Farmer's Llamas
programme.desc: When the Farmer and Bitzer go to a country fair, Shaun the Sheep steals away with them intent on causing mischief. He spies an auction where he sees three exotic and very crafty llamas going under the hammer. Instantly impressed by their amusing pranks, Shaun sneakily gets the Farmer to buy them, but it's not long before these cheeky newcomers wreak havoc. Animated fun, with the voice of Justin Fletcher

programme.title: Zootropolis (2016)
programme.desc: In a city populated by animals, a rabbit achieves her dream of becoming the first bunny to become a police officer - but faces prejudice at every turn. When the disappearance of an otter hints at a terrible conspiracy in the city, she enlists the aid of a con-artist fox in solving the mystery. Disney animated comedy, with the voices of Ginnifer Goodwin, Jason Bateman and Idris Elba

programme.title: Frozen (2013)
programme.desc: A princess with the power to freeze anything she touches loses control of her abilities and plunges the kingdom into a perpetual winter. She goes into hiding, but her sister remains determined that she can be saved from her own magic and sets off on a quest to find her, aided by a mountain man and a living snowman. Disney animated fantasy adventure, with the voices of Kristen Bell and Idina Menzel

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present another edition of their unorthodox, star-filled quiz show. This time, all the contestants are from the world of music, and as befits the time of year, the entry requirement is that they must have released a successful Christmas hit. Stop the Cavalry singer Jona Lewie teams up with Mike Batt, who released Wombling Merry Christmas, while 1972 Christmas Number One hit-maker Jimmy Osmond pairs with his 2015 counterpart - X Factor's Ben Haenow. Choirmaster Gareth Malone joins forces with Samantha Stevenson of the Military Wives Choir, and Justin and Dan Hawkins of The Darkness also compete, before playing out the show with their festive favourite Christmas Time (Don't Let the Bells End)

programme.title: Penguins: Meet the Family
programme.desc: Documentary following penguins living in unexpected environments, including satellite imagery of previously undiscovered colonies of flightless birds. Among their habitats are dense forests, desert burrows and city streets. The programme reveals the challenges they face in making their homes in these challenging environments, from evading predators to scaling rocks, and captures the lifelong bonds formed between family groups

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Patrick is desperate to help Denise and takes matters into his own hands - although he may have only made matters worse. Ruby warns Stacey to stay out of her marriage, while Jay makes out that the kiss with Honey was a mistake - is he hiding his true feelings?

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The first of two special Christmas episodes of the culinary challenge, in which eight finalists and semi-finalists from past years return to face a festive test of their skills. The first episode features Danny Parker from the 2014 series, 2017's Louisa Ellis, 2009's Marianne Lumb, and 2018 semi-finalist William Chilila. Monica Galetti sets them the task of making festive canapés, before they move on to the dreaded skills test and a seasonally themed two-course menu

programme.title: Black Narcissus
programme.desc: Sister Adela and Father Roberts prove unlikely allies for Ruth, forcing Clodagh to impose strict order or risk losing control of the convent, as well as herself. But events escalate as Dilip Rai and Kanchi are discovered together, with dramatic consequences. Ruth flees the palace to declare her love to Mr Dean, who is defending the convent against retaliation from the village. Conclusion of the adaptation of Rumer Godden's novel, starring Gemma Arterton and Alessandro Nivola

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League fixtures, including Manchester United v Wolverhampton Wanderers at Old Trafford and Brighton & Hove Albion v Arsenal at Amex Stadium. United and Wolves met on four occasions in all competitions last term, with three of those contests ending level and the Red Devils prevailing 1-0 at home in an FA Cup replay in the other. Brighton did the double over the Gunners last season, winning 2-1 home and away, and will be hopeful of shocking the Londoners once again. Plus, a round-up of the rest of tonight and yesterday's fixtures

programme.title: Point Break (1991)
programme.desc: An FBI agent infiltrates a Californian surfing gang suspected of carrying out a series of armed robberies wearing masks modelled on former US presidents. However, his loyalties are tested to the limits when he is befriended by the gang's leader. Kathryn Bigelow's thriller, starring Keanu Reeves, Patrick Swayze, Gary Busey and Lori Petty

programme.title: This Country
programme.desc: The annual harvest festival brings the whole village together, but one resident's announcement causes unexpected emotional consequences for the community. Spoof documentary exploring modern life in the Cotswolds, written by and starring siblings Daisy May and Charlie Cooper

programme.title: Anita & Me (2002)
programme.desc: Twelve-year-old Meena strikes up an unlikely friendship with blonde teenage tearaway Anita. Despite their ethnic and social differences, the girls manage to support each other through difficult times while their families cope with life's problems. Comedy drama set in a Midlands village in the 1970s and based on the novel by Meera Syal, starring Chandeep Uppal, Anna Brewster, Max Beesley, Sanjeev Bhaskar, Kathy Burke, Lynn Redgrave and Ayesha Dharker

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: A to Z of TV Gardening
programme.desc: Carol Kirkwood looks at themes beginning with the letter B, as part of her alphabetical journey through the world of horticulture. Along the way, she gets inspiration and advice from some of the BBC's most popular gardening presenters

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The team celebrates Christmas on the Auchlyne estate in Scotland. Charlotte Smith and Joe Crowley join laird Emma and her family to help monitor beavers and tame some deer. Matt Baker makes decorations for a special Christmas tree that will help birds survive the winter cold, and John Craven finds out about the ancient tradition of Gaelic psalm singing

programme.title: A Wild Year
programme.desc: The seasons are marked on Wales' Pembrokeshire's coast by the arrival of the puffins, which court by tapping their multi-coloured bills. Farmers grow the earliest potatoes, while sheep are employed to maintain the clifftop pastures for the rare choughs that use their long red beaks to probe for grubs. As autumn arrives, the grey seals produce their white-coated pups just before the gales mark the might of winter. Using time-lapse techniques, this documentary brings fresh insight into the restless rhythms of the countryside in an exploration of how and why this region is the way it is. Narrated by Toby Jones

programme.title: Sailing
programme.desc: The best of the action from the world of sailing

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt takes a look back at some of his favourite recipes and best moments from Saturday Kitchen

programme.title: Nadiya's Party Feasts
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain shares her favourite festive dishes, making the perfect food to hand around and share, such as melt-in-the-mouth halloumi chips, smoked mackerel pate choux and sticky lamb ribs. She also prepares a hearty dive-in dish of beef chilli and jalapeno corn bread and some vibrant salads - Brussels sprout slaw and pomegranate and parsley tabbouleh. Plus, for dessert a chocolate eclair roll and passion fruit bundt. The accomplished cook also joins a group of children as they make cupcakes, lends a hand with a trifle and helps out with a surprise birthday cake

programme.title: Nature's Weirdest Events
programme.desc: Chris Packham investigates more unusual natural events, with the help of eyewitness accounts, scientists and footage of the occurrences. He examines the cases of a town brought to a standstill by a swarm of flying insects, polar bears whose bizarre friendships shocked a community and residents kept awake by deafening mating calls

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: Sylvia Syms revisits some of the great British war films that helped boost the nation's morale in the difficult years that followed the end of the Second World War. Among the silver screen gems highlighted are In Which We Serve, Reach for the Sky, Bridge on the River Kwai and The Dam Busters. The programme includes archive interviews with stars including Noel Coward, Richard Attenborough, David Lean, Alec Guinness, Kenneth More and John Mills, and Sylvia shares her memories of filming her own contribution to the genre, Ice Cold in Alex

programme.title: Operation Crossbow (1965)
programme.desc: Three Allied engineers parachute into occupied Holland on a mission to infiltrate and destroy a German missile base. The trio team up with the local resistance, but two of the men are unaware the third is a Nazi agent. Second World War adventure, starring George Peppard, Sophia Loren, Trevor Howard, Tom Courtenay and John Mills

programme.title: The Heroes of Telemark (1965)
programme.desc: German scientists in occupied Norway during the Second World War begin work on the manufacture of vital components for an atomic bomb, prompting the Norwegian resistance to team up with British forces on a campaign of sabotage to halt production. Second World War thriller, starring Kirk Douglas, Richard Harris, Anton Diffring, Roy Dotrice and Ulla Jacobsson

programme.title: Great British Christmas Menu
programme.desc: The show reaches its climax as the winners prepare for the ultimate Christmas feast. But when a second wave makes a traditional banquet impossible, Andi Oliver and the winning chefs formulate a plan to make sure they deliver on their promise to celebrate the nation's key workers. If the workers cannot come to the banquet, they will take it to them, hosting six mini-dinners - and giving others a 'banquet-in-a-box' to cook at home

programme.title: The Snow Wolf: A Winter's Tale
programme.desc: Dramatised natural history film, following an alpha female wolf in the Dolomite mountains. A tragic sequence of events forces the wolf to flee her pack while pregnant in search of new territory. Along the way, she gives birth to six cubs, which she strives to protect from humans and predators as she journeys into the Alps in search of a new home. Narrated by Emilia Fox

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace visits the biggest cider factory in the world, which produces more than 350 million litres each year. Cherry Healey explores how the factory is creating new orchards of a sweet apple variety called Scrumptious. And historian Ruth Goodman traces a Victorian apple breeding boom with Dr Annie Gray, recreating one of Queen Victoria's favourite baked apple desserts

programme.title: Last Woman on Earth with Sara Pascoe
programme.desc: The comedian travels the world to meet people doing rare crafts and jobs that are rapidly becoming lost skills, and tries to pick up their unusual talents. Her journey begins in Cuba, where she finds many professions are being put at risk by increased commercialism and meets a mattress magician, a cigar factory reader and a family who climb giant coconut trees to make a local sweet

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig hosts a festive edition of the panel game, joined by Chris McCausland, Justin Moorhouse, Holly Walsh and regular Alan Davies. The panel is posed questions on topics like how to get a reindeer into a submarine, why the children of Dijon rebelled against Santa Claus and what the immaculate conception really means. Along the way, the show sets two Christmas-themed world records

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: A special edition of the topical comedy show featuring seasonal material, fond favourites and previously unseen bits, with host Dara O Briain and regular panellist Hugh Dennis

programme.title: Sara Pascoe Live: LadsLadsLads
programme.desc: The comedian performs the finale of her critically acclaimed 2017 show at the London Palladium, charting her efforts to be happily single after the break-up of a four-year relationship and oversharing about her imaginary brother Stephen

programme.title: The Ranganation
programme.desc: Romesh Ranganathan hosts a topical comedy show, in which a diverse group of 25 members of the public drawn from all walks of life - including Romesh's mother - are invited to share their thoughts on the state of modern Britain today and the latest talking points in the media. Romesh and his outspoken and passionate focus group will be joined in the studio by a selection of celebrity guests, comedians and experts, to investigate how and why the events in the news matter to people across the country

programme.title: The Ranganation
programme.desc: Romesh Ranganathan hosts a topical comedy show, in which a diverse group of 25 members of the public drawn from all walks of life are invited to share their thoughts on the state of modern Britain today. Romesh and his outspoken and passionate focus group will be joined in the studio by Katherine Ryan

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: Nathan and Tracy face a fight for their lives after being cornered and trapped by a monstrous Suresh as he tries to keeps his test subjects under control - and Matt has his own problems when Daphne is sent to kill him. Elle returns with a shock for Claire, while Angela uses Sylar to rescue Peter from Pinehearst. Hiro questions African precognitive Usutu's advice on how to battle his gathering opponents

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: Hiro goes into the past to explore what turned Arthur Petrelli, Sylar and Flint into villains. Beginning by following Arthur, he discovers why the once-respected businessman created Pinehearst. Then, a scene unfolds where a guilt-ridden Sylar might have stopped his murderous pursuit of power. Finally, under the wing of Company man Thompson, agent-in-training Meredith watches her new life go up in smoke after the arrival of her brother Flint

programme.title: Great British Christmas Menu
programme.desc: Chefs Simon Rogan and Tommy Banks join the desserts and petit fours round, in which the eight contestants prepare dishes including edible snow globes, mince pies, baked Alaska, and a new taker on the classic Christmas selection box. They must also judge each other's efforts in a blind taste test. Former judge Andi Oliver hosts

programme.title: Great British Christmas Menu
programme.desc: The top three chefs from the dessert and petit fours heats cook for the judges, hoping to secure their place as part of the special festive feast in honour of Britain's key workers. This week, actor and comedian Kerry Godliman and regular judges Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton are joined by special guest judge, Beverly Knight MBE. Hosted by Andi Oliver

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: A Very Country Christmas
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison and Angellica Bell celebrate Cornish Christmas traditions, visiting the cathedral where Nine Lessons and Carols first began - with the intention of getting people out of pubs and into church

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: The teams have reached the halfway point of their journey. For the next leg, must choose whether to travel through Bolivia or Chile - both countries with unstable political climates. Sam and Jo decide to break up their travel with regular breaks, while Jen and Rob have an explosive argument when they get caught up in Bolivia's general election. Emon and Jamiul find work in an Argentinian salt mine, and Dom has a health scare that may require him to pull out of the race

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Weasels are often portrayed as villains and associated with bad behaviour. In an attempt to determine whether that assessment is correct, this documentary employs the use of over 50 hidden cameras to follow the adventures of first-time stoat mum Bandita as she raises her kits in a unique weasel wonderland. Cameras capture the creatures interacting, revealing the true nature of the weasel family - feisty and fearless. Narrated by Julie Walters

programme.title: Evil Under the Sun (1982)
programme.desc: Hercule Poirot braves the heat and sand to investigate the murder of a glamorous stage star on an idyllic Adriatic holiday island. It becomes clear many people had reason to want the temperamental actress dead and the Belgian sleuth is soon hard at work figuring out which of her enemies eventually managed to kill her. Mystery, with Peter Ustinov, Diana Rigg, James Mason, Maggie Smith and Jane Birkin

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: As Kenneth Branagh prepares to unveil the latest version of Death on the Nile, Celia Imrie narrates a look at how Agatha Christie's murder mysteries have been treated by movie-makers over the years

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: To celebrate 20 years of the programme, Eric Knowles takes a look back at some of the best and worst buys the reds and blues have bought over two decades

programme.title: Grand Tours of the Scottish Islands
programme.desc: Paul Murton explores the Isle of Mull and its satellite islands, to discover why they have become boltholes from the hustle and bustle of the modern world

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A look at TV appearances made over the years by actor Sean Connery, capturing the milestones and highlights of his life and career. Narrated by Sylvia Syms

programme.title: Murder on the Orient Express (1974)
programme.desc: An American businessman travelling on the Orient Express from Istanbul to Paris tries to hire Hercule Poirot to discover the source of the death threats that have been made against him. The sleuth refuses, but wakes up the following morning to learn the man has been stabbed to death while the train was held up by heavy snow - and there is no shortage of suspects among the passengers. Agatha Christie mystery, starring Albert Finney, Lauren Bacall, Ingrid Bergman, Sean Connery and Vanessa Redgrave

programme.title: Death on the Nile (1978)
programme.desc: Detective Hercule Poirot investigates a murder on board a paddle steamer in Egypt. The victim was an unpopular heiress, whose outspoken manner put her at odds with most of her fellow passengers during their cruise on the Nile. Agatha Christie mystery, starring Peter Ustinov, Bette Davis, Maggie Smith, George Kennedy, Mia Farrow, Jane Birkin, Olivia Hussey, Angela Lansbury and David Niven

programme.title: Michael Palin: Travels of a Lifetime
programme.desc: Michael Palin revisits his travel series, beginning with Around the World in 80 Days. He draws on his personal archive of audio recordings and diary notebooks, and reflects on how he became a seasoned global traveller. The first episode revisits his visits to Venice, Cairo, Saudi Arabia, India, Hong Kong and China - a nation that was closed to most westerners at the time. Featuring contributions from fans and friends including David Attenborough, Joanna Lumley and Simon Reeve

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys invites four more contestants to brave the black chair, asking them questions on the specialist subjects of Kenneth Williams, UK hit singles 2000-2004, Ada Lovelace, and the Rugby World Cup, before they are tested on their general knowledge

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell hosts the third second-round match as the Barons take on the Dungeon Masters for a place in the quarter-finals. The contestants must use patience, lateral thinking and sheer inspiration to make connections between four things that may appear at first not to be linked

programme.title: Christmas University Challenge
programme.desc: Teams made up of distinguished graduates of Durham University and Downing College, Cambridge compete to secure a place in the semi-finals. Among those facing Jeremy Paxman's questions are weather presenter Sarah Keith-Lucas and comedian Ed Gamble for Durham, with the BBC's media editor Amol Rajan and its global trade correspondent Dharshini David. representing Downing

programme.title: Two Doors Down
programme.desc: The neighbours head off on a rare trip away from Latimer Crescent, but the break at a luxury lodge in the Highlands threatens to derail their friendship when Cathy pushes Beth too far. Comedy, starring Arabella Weir, Alex Norton, Doon Mackichan, Jonathan Watson and Elaine C Smith

programme.title: Charlie Brooker's Antiviral Wipe
programme.desc: The writer and broadcaster returns to front his own show since the award-winning Charlie Brooker's 2016 Wipe, to take a satirical look at life under lockdown. As well as the crisis itself, he will be finding out what people have been watching to while away the hours, with help from the ever-insightful Philomena Cunk

programme.title: Secrets of the Apollo
programme.desc: Comedians who have appeared on Live at the Apollo over the show's first 16 years take a seat in the stalls of the famous Hammersmith theatre, and share their memories of the series. New interviews with the comics provide a unique insight into how it feels to take to British TV's most famous stand-up stage, alongside classic archive clips from the show

programme.title: Liam Gallagher: As It Was (2019)
programme.desc: Documentary about the former Oasis frontman. The film by directors Charlie Lightening and Gavin Fitzgerald follows his journey, from the dizzying heights of superstardom with his brother Noel in the 1990s, to years in the musical wilderness, before his hugely successful comeback as a solo artist in recent years

programme.title: The Babadook (2014)
programme.desc: A widowed mother's life becomes increasingly stressful when her son becomes convinced that the monster in one of his books is real. As she struggles to cope with the boy's unusual behaviour and her own sense of isolation, she begins to share his belief in the creature. Horror, starring Essie Davis and Noah Wiseman

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The team celebrates Christmas on the Auchlyne estate in Scotland. Charlotte Smith and Joe Crowley join laird Emma and her family to help monitor beavers and tame some deer. Matt Baker makes decorations for a special Christmas tree that will help birds survive the winter cold, and John Craven finds out about the ancient tradition of Gaelic psalm singing

programme.title: Great British Christmas Menu
programme.desc: The show reaches its climax as the winners prepare for the ultimate Christmas feast. But when a second wave makes a traditional banquet impossible, Andi Oliver and the winning chefs formulate a plan to make sure they deliver on their promise to celebrate the nation's key workers. If the workers cannot come to the banquet, they will take it to them, hosting six mini-dinners - and giving others a 'banquet-in-a-box' to cook at home

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: Finals week gets underway with the last Invention Test of the year. The six remaining contestants are divided into two teams, one of which prepares rabbit in mustard sauce, while the other makes bouillabaisse with a rouille and a crouton. The chef who presents the finest dish in each of the groups will be rewarded with a pass straight through to the final four, while the others must make a classic dessert to avoid elimination

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Nadiya's American Adventure
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain presents this two-part documentary in which she explores Louisiana and California to find out how generations of immigrant communities have cultivated and adapted American cuisine. Her Louisiana food adventure starts in New Orleans on the first day of the Mardi Gras, where she bakes some indulgent king cakes bursting with chantilly cream, before joining a lively street party. She also visits an African American neighbourhood devastated by Hurricane Katrina, meeting a man who has spent his life savings to open a grocery store and soul food cafe, and the swamps of Cajun Country, where she learns how the locals live off the land

programme.title: Amazing Hotels: Life Beyond the Lobby
programme.desc: Giles Coren and Monica Galetti travel to Kulm Hotel St Moritz. This Swiss Alpine hotel was the birthplace of winter tourism more than 150 years ago. Back then the owner made a bet with summer guests that if they returned in the winter and did not enjoy themselves he would pay for their stay. The guests stayed for the season - and a new industry of winter leisure and sports was born. During their stay, Giles works as a bellboy alongside veteran head concierge Silvio, while Monica helps joint managing director Jenny transform her office into a private dining room for a nostalgic candlelit dinner

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: The teams prepare to leave the checkpoint in Cafayate, Argentina, except for Dom and Lizzie, whose disastrous last leg has left them more than 24 hours behind their competitors. The next destination is the Brazilian island of Ilha Grande, halfway between Rio de Janeiro and Sao Paolo, and at 3,600 kilometres, it is the longest stage of the journey. Emon and Jamiul decide to travel through Paraguay, while Jen and Rob decide they need to see more of the continent, and stop off at the famous Iguazu Falls

programme.title: 12 Puppies and Us
programme.desc: Woody the St Bernard moves in with five-year-old Dexter, who has cerebral palsy. Dexter's family, in Surrey, are hoping that the little pup will become his companion and learn to carry out simple assistance tasks. In Hull, Mungo joins Alastair, Daron and their possessive older cocker spaniel Milo - but the dogs clash from day one, and Alastair and Daron call on Louise to help

programme.title: The Bear Family and Me
programme.desc: Wildlife cameraman Gordon Buchanan follows a family of wild black bears for a year. He arrives in the forests of Minnesota in spring to observe the animals emerging from their dens. However, before he can start filming, he faces the challenge of earning their trust and overcoming his own fear of the creatures, which have a dangerous reputation

programme.title: Escape to the Perfect Town
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin offers advice to house hunters who want to move to the lively market town of Abergavenny in Monmouthshire

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Two teams of experts compete as veterans Thomas Forrester, Philip Serrell and Kate Bliss take on more recent additions Tim Weeks, Danny Sebastian and Roo Irvine. As a team they each have one hour and £300 to buys three items to take to auction, but to turn up the heat they will also be shopping on their own in a relay. Presented by Natasha Raskin Sharp

programme.title: Nature's Weirdest Events
programme.desc: Chris Packham investigates unusual natural events, with the help of eyewitness accounts, scientists and footage of the occurrences

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Port Lockroy on Goudier Island in the British Antarctic Territory is the site of one of the world's most remote post offices, which has a staff of four and is open to visiting tourists during the Antarctic summer. They share the neighbourhood with more than 3,000 gentoo penguins, and this documentary follows the trials and tribulations of the birds as they try to raise their chicks in these unusual surroundings. Juliet Stevenson narrates

programme.title: Love & Friendship (2016)
programme.desc: Lady Susan Vernon arrives at her brother-in-law's residence, Churchill, with a matchmaking plan for her daughter, having left a trail of confusion behind her at her last residence, Manwaring. While young Reginald de Courcey is charmed by her, some of her family are deeply suspicious of her motives. But few people are equal to her schemes. Period comedy based on Jane Austen's novella Lady Susan, starring Kate Beckinsale, Chloe Sevigny and Stepheny Fry

programme.title: Emma (1996)
programme.desc: A privileged, headstrong young woman, who believes she is an expert in matters of the heart, sets about matchmaking between her friends - little realising she is falling in love herself and risks missing out on a chance of happiness. Period drama adapted from Jane Austen's novel, starring Gwyneth Paltrow, Ewan McGregor, Jeremy Northam, Toni Collette, Greta Scacchi and Juliet Stevenson

programme.title: Earth's Tropical Islands
programme.desc: In the heart of south-east Asia lies the tropical island of Borneo. Twice the size of the British Isles, it is the third largest island on earth and home to possibly the greatest diversity of life of any island. Its huge variety of habitats, from bustling coral reefs and ancient jungles to towering mountains, have given rise to more than 60,000 species of plants and animals - many found nowhere else on Earth. This documentary covers Borneo's rich abundance of wildlife, from frogs to orang-utans

programme.title: Michael Palin: Travels of a Lifetime
programme.desc: Michael Palin looks back on the making of Pole to Pole, in which he travelled along a single line of longitude across the USSR, Africa and finally into Antarctica. Along the way, he paid a visit to Chernobyl and gained an insight into the last days of the Soviet Union. His travels across Africa led to an encounter with some fellow globetrotters from his home town of Sheffield - but he was forced to make a detour when his planned route passed through a war zone

programme.title: Inside Monaco: Playground of the Rich
programme.desc: Fred Sirieix narrates a documentary giving an insight into the principality on the French Riviera, with access to the royal palace as well as the super-rich and those who serve them. In the first edition, director Michael Waldman explores how this extraordinary country operates, meeting everyone from Prince Albert and his staff to wealthy widow Monika Bacardi, who takes him on a tour in her chauffeur-driven classic Rolls-Royce. He also goes track side with ex-Formula One driver David Coulthard and team owner Eddie Jordan during the Monaco Grand Prix

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell presents the first of four festive episodes of the quiz show. Two favourite family teams from previous series, the Edwards and the Wrights, return for a family-themed episode. They put their patience and lateral thinking to the test as they strive to make connections between things that at first do not appear to be linked

programme.title: Christmas University Challenge
programme.desc: Jeremy Paxman hosts the last first round match, with teams of famous alumni from New College Oxford and Reading competing for a place in the semi-finals. The New College team includes political broadcaster Michael Crick and writer and playwright Christopher Hampton, while Newsnight culture correspondent Stephen Smith and film and TV critic Rhianna Dhillon are playing for Reading

programme.title: Celebrity: A 21st Century Story
programme.desc: The world of celebrity has been transformed since the turn of the century, driven by some of the most dramatic technological and cultural developments in recent times. The first episode examines how the Beckhams changed the model of the celebrity power couple, while the rise of reality TV led to a gigantic influx of people gaining overnight fame, fed by a press that saw an easy source of material. The programme also examines how leaked sex tapes changed the nature of celebrity scandal

programme.title: Take That: We've Come a Long Way
programme.desc: As Take That mark their 30th year, this programme celebrates one of Britain's best-loved boy bands, with help from fans from all over the world sharing their stories of how the group touched their lives. Robbie Williams rejoins Gary Barlow, Howard Donald and Mark Owen to share favourite memories, there's a glimpse of preparations for their anniversary album, and footage of them in the studio with Bee Gees veteran Barry Gibb

programme.title: The Story of Pulp's Common People
programme.desc: Documentary examining the origins of the indie band's song about class differences in the 1990s. Frontman Jarvis Cocker returns to St Martin's College, where he met the girl who became the inspiration for the track's heroine, while the band recall the single's success. With contributions from Sadie Frost, Vic Reeves, Franz Ferdinand and Kaiser Chiefs

programme.title: The Ranganation
programme.desc: Romesh Ranganathan hosts a topical comedy show, in which a diverse group of 25 members of the public drawn from all walks of life are invited to share their thoughts on the state of modern Britain today. Romesh and his outspoken and passionate focus group will be joined in the studio by Jon Richardson

programme.title: The Ranganation
programme.desc: Romesh Ranganathan hosts a topical comedy show, in which a diverse group of 25 members of the public drawn from all walks of life are invited to share their thoughts on the state of modern Britain today. Romesh and his outspoken and passionate focus group will be joined in the studio by a selection of celebrity guests, including Sara Pascoe

programme.title: Happy New Year, Colin Burstead (2018)
programme.desc: A man plans to prove his worth to his extended family by throwing them a lavish New Year party, and rents a stately home in the country for the occasion. However, his status as the man of the hour is threatened by the unexpected reappearance of his estranged brother after a five-year absence, a development that throws all the relationships in the family into upheaval. Comedy directed by Ben Wheatley (High Rise, Sightseers), starring Neil Maskell, Sam Riley, Hayley Squires, Charles Dance and Doon Mackichan

programme.title: New Elizabethans with Andrew Marr
programme.desc: The final documentary looks at one of the greatest challenges faced by modern Elizabethans - the loss of Britain's manufacturing heartlands and the surprising impact it has had on the state of the nation. Andrew Marr selects a rich cast of characters, from Christopher Cockerell to Clive Sinclair, who in his opinion have responded to the challenges and found ingenious ways of adapting to the changing industrial landscape

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The remaining four chefs embark on the penultimate round of the competition, cooking for some of the most respected chefs in the UK at the Chefs Table. The chefs have three hours and 15 minutes to create a spectacular dish and get it to the pass ready to serve, hoping to wow the judges and earn praise from the best in the business. In the second round, the competitors are challenged to create a dish of their own design dedicated to someone special in their life, before one of them is sent home

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: London to Brighton: Side By Side
programme.desc: In 1953, the BBC commissioned a recording filmed from the point-of-view of a London to Brighton train. In 1983, another film of the same journey was recorded. This short programme presents these two iconic recordings side-by-side, demonstrating how both locomotive technology and the landscape have evolved over the years

programme.title: Proms on Four: Friday Night at the Proms
programme.desc: The conductor and his orchestra celebrate the music of Hollywood at London's Royal Albert Hall with extracts from film scores including Bernard Herrmann's Psycho, Erich Korngold's Robin Hood, Max Steiner's Casablanca and Miklos Rozsa's Ben Hur. The concert, from the 2013 season of Proms, also features a medley of theme songs from otherwise non-musical movies, performed by vocalists Venera Gimadieva, Matthew Ford and Jane Monheit. Presented by Katie Derham

programme.title: Billy Elliot (2000)
programme.desc: A northern youngster is pushed into taking boxing lessons by his macho dad - but secretly enrols in a ballet class instead, where it soon becomes clear he has a real talent for fancy footwork. However, he faces a tough time convincing his father that dance is a worthwhile pursuit. Comedy drama, starring Jamie Bell, Julie Walters and Gary Lewis

programme.title: Singin' in the Rain (1952)
programme.desc: A silent-movie star tries to win the heart of an aspiring actress as the introduction of sound changes the film business for ever. However, his frequent on-screen love interest does not take kindly to this real-life romance. Musical comedy, starring Gene Kelly, Debbie Reynolds, Donald O'Connor, Jean Hagen and Millard Mitchell. Songs include Make 'em Laugh, You Are My Lucky Star and the title number

programme.title: Sings Bacharach and David!
programme.desc: Archive performances of songs penned by Burt Bacharach and Hal David, featuring artists including Aretha Franklin, Dusty Springfield, the Carpenters, Sandie Shaw and the Stranglers. The programme also features Bacharach himself performing alongside Rufus Wainwright on Later with Jools Holland

programme.title: Betroffenheit from Sadler's Wells
programme.desc: Filmed performance of the award-winning theatrical production from London's Sadler's Wells Theatre. The show was created by choreographer Crystal Pite and writer Jonathan Young, who also performs in the show, and explores themes of trauma, grief and addiction through dance, spoken word, music and puppetry, drawing inspiration from the deaths of members of Young's own family

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson examines the history of Lindisfarne, which lies off the Northumberland coast. From what became known as the Holy Island of Lindisfarne, monks spread Christianity to the rest of northern England and created one of Britain's finest treasures - the Lindisfarne Gospels. Viking attacks caused the monks to flee but the village survived and today the community has to share it with thousands of tourists

programme.title: The Joy of Painting: Winter Specials
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross Bob Ross paints a scene featuring an isolated cabin located deep in the woods, on a cold, snowy winter's day

programme.title: Royal Institution Christmas Lectures 2020
programme.desc: The first of three lectures on the hidden wonders of Earth and the impact of human activity, with Professor Chris Jackson charting the planet's changing climate. He shows how the oldest rocks and fossils provide evidence of radical climate changes throughout its history, revealing that what drives them is the amount of carbon dioxide in the atmosphere

programme.title: Kenneth Williams: Fantabulosa!
programme.desc: Michael Sheen takes the lead role in a drama based on Kenneth Williams' diaries. The film charts the comic actor's professional successes, which included the Carry On series, radio programmes such as Round the Horne and numerous TV appearances. It also delves behind the laughter to explore the actor's problems, insecurity and self-doubt

programme.title: Comic Roots
programme.desc: A 1983 documentary in which Kenneth Williams explores his roots in and around London's St Pancras district where he grew up

programme.title: How Auld Lang Syne Took Over the World
programme.desc: Dougie Vipond examines how Robert Burns' 18th-century poem, traditionally sung around the world on New Year's Eve, became one of Scotland's best-known musical exports. From Ayrshire to Tokyo, via New York City, he finds out how Auld Lang Syne has been adopted by different cultures, with commentary from well-known faces including Alan Cumming, Cliff Richard and Clare Grogan

programme.title: Marianne and Leonard: Words of Love (2019)
programme.desc: The lives of composer Leonard Cohen and his Norwegian muse Marianne Ihlen, whose romance began on the Greek island of Hydra in 1960 as part of a bohemian community of foreign artists, writers, and musicians. This film follows their relationship, from the early days on Hydra, a humble time of 'free love' and open marriage, to how their love evolved when Leonard became a successful musician. Along the way the film tells of the tragedy that befell those that could not survive the beauty of Hydra, the highs and lows of Leonard's career, and the inspirational power that Marianne possessed

programme.title: The Joy of Painting: Winter Specials
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross Bob Ross paints a scene featuring an isolated cabin located deep in the woods, on a cold, snowy winter's day

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson examines the history of Lindisfarne, which lies off the Northumberland coast. From what became known as the Holy Island of Lindisfarne, monks spread Christianity to the rest of northern England and created one of Britain's finest treasures - the Lindisfarne Gospels. Viking attacks caused the monks to flee but the village survived and today the community has to share it with thousands of tourists

programme.title: Kenneth Williams: Fantabulosa!
programme.desc: Michael Sheen takes the lead role in a drama based on Kenneth Williams' diaries. The film charts the comic actor's professional successes, which included the Carry On series, radio programmes such as Round the Horne and numerous TV appearances. It also delves behind the laughter to explore the actor's problems, insecurity and self-doubt

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson examines the history of Robin Hood's Bay, the fishing village on the North Yorkshire coast, revealing tales of smuggling intrigue and bravery

programme.title: The Joy of Painting: Winter Specials
programme.desc: Bob Ross demonstrates how to paint an intriguing yet simple painting of an old log cabin nestled deep in the snowy woods

programme.title: Royal Institution Christmas Lectures 2020
programme.desc: Dr Helen Czerski reveals how shifting ocean water distributes heat and nutrients around the Earth. This process is linked to almost every aspect of human existence, and the lecture explores how dependent we are on the ocean for weather, food supply and connection between land masses - as well as exploring what needs to be done by humans to maintain this vital element of the Earth's ecosystem

programme.title: Bruce Dickinson: Scream for Me Sarajevo (2017)
programme.desc: Documentary about a 1994 concert by Bruce Dickinson and his band Skunkworks in Sarajevo, when the capital of Bosnia and Herzegovina was in its third year under siege during the ongoing Bosnian War. Featuring personal accounts of the band members who risked their lives to perform in a war zone, and the fans who attended the concert while living in the midst of continual violence

programme.title: Metal Britannia
programme.desc: Documentary tracing the genre's emergence in the late 1960s, pioneered by bands including Black Sabbath and Judas Priest, who were based in the industrialised Midlands. This classic form of metal was challenged by punk in the late 1970s, before a new wave of groups such as Iron Maiden, Def Leppard and Saxon reinvigorated the style. Featuring contributions by singers Ian Gillan, Glenn Tipton, Lemmy, Bruce Dickinson and Rob Halford, guitarist Tony Iommi and keyboard player Jon Lord. Narrated by Nigel Planer

programme.title: Metallica at Glastonbury 2014
programme.desc: A chance to see the band's headline set on the Pyramid Stage at the 2014 event - the first time a heavy metal group had topped the bill at the famous festival. Throughout their 90-minute set they played tracks from their back catalogue including Nothing Else Matters, Enter Sandman, Seek and Destroy plus Thin Lizzy's Whiskey in the Jar

programme.title: The Joy of Painting: Winter Specials
programme.desc: Bob Ross demonstrates how to paint an intriguing yet simple painting of an old log cabin nestled deep in the snowy woods

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson examines the history of Robin Hood's Bay, the fishing village on the North Yorkshire coast, revealing tales of smuggling intrigue and bravery

programme.title: Royal Institution Christmas Lectures 2020
programme.desc: The first of three lectures on the hidden wonders of Earth and the impact of human activity, with Professor Chris Jackson charting the planet's changing climate. He shows how the oldest rocks and fossils provide evidence of radical climate changes throughout its history, revealing that what drives them is the amount of carbon dioxide in the atmosphere

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: Animated antics with the adventurous aardvark and his friends

programme.title: Shaun the Sheep
programme.desc: The Farmer's new hobby leads to him getting stuck in a tree, and needs Blitzer to rescue him

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: When the crimebusting rodent and sidekick Penfold are sent on a rescue mission to a mysterious island, DM's over-competitiveness borders on criminal

programme.title: Dennis & Gnasher Unleashed!
programme.desc: Dennis discovers that Ralf, the janitor, is the legendary Prankmaster General and is told he could become his successor. Walter tries to prevent this happening and challenges Dennis to a prank battle to win Ralf's book of pranking - the infamous Prankypedia

programme.title: The Zoo
programme.desc: It is the day of the zoo's annual stocktake, but as all the animals get ready to be counted, a group of tiny frogs takes the opportunity to escape to Paris

programme.title: Odd Squad
programme.desc: The agents try to determine what kind of creature will hatch from an egg

programme.title: Danny & Mick
programme.desc: Danny is convinced it's Joy's birthday and he sets about organising a surprise party

programme.title: Gangsta Granny
programme.desc: Family comedy based on the children's novel by David Walliams. Schoolboy Ben is bored witless after being made to stay with his grandma. All she ever wants to do is play Scrabble and eat yucky cabbage soup. But it turns out there's a whole lot more to this OAP than cardigans and toffees when she tells him how she used to be an international jewel thief - and when she admits there was one heist she never quite managed in her heyday, the pair set out on a criminal adventure. Julia McKenzie stars as the shady senior citizen, with Walliams and Miranda Hart as the boys' ballroom-dancing parents, alongside Joanna Lumley, Rob Brydon and Robbie Williams, with Reece Buttery as Ben

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Long-running children's magazine programme

programme.title: Gym Stars
programme.desc: Artistic gymnast Amelie represents Team GB for her first European Champs as a senior.

programme.title: Horrible Histories
programme.desc: Compilation of characters from around the globe, including Genghis Khan and Confucius, as well as a dance-off during a naval battle and a Bollywood number from Queen Victoria

programme.title: Horrible Histories
programme.desc: The life and times of Queen Victoria, following her as she ascends the throne at 18, marries her true love Albert, and has to put up with the open sewers at Buckingham Palace. The programme also catches up with Australian outlaw Ned Kelly, and discovers plans for the Great Hedge of India. Starring Sarah Hadland

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve sets sail to Bali for a diving adventure to find a giant ocean sunfish

programme.title: All Over the Place
programme.desc: This time it's an exclusive playlist of some of the very best songs from the series

programme.title: Operation Ouch!
programme.desc: Series about the human body, exploring how it works and how it recovers when injured

programme.title: Operation Ouch!
programme.desc: Series about the human body, exploring how it works and how it recovers when injured

programme.title: Mystic
programme.desc: Issie gets a new vision of Mystic running through the forest along an old railway track, and wonders if this might be the clue that will lead her to Blaze’s herd. After some encouragement, Issie decides to ride out on Blaze to find the tracks, and Caroline, Caleb, Dan and Natasha join her on the mission. As they journey deeper into the forest, past their favourite swimming hole, the kids make a series of bewildering discoveries and eventually realise they’re completely and utterly lost. With one of the gang’s future ambitions at stake, they face a race against time to get back to the stables. Despite their best efforts to hide what happened, the consequences of their mission have serious consequences for them all as Tom presents the group with a difficult dilemma. Who will be able to ride in the Cross-Country Trials?

programme.title: Odd Squad: The Movie ()
programme.desc: When a group of adults runs Odd Squad out of business, the agents are forced to become regular kids again

programme.title: Crackerjack!
programme.desc: Sam and Mark host a bumper edition of festive fun! Naga Munchetty and Charlie Stayt play an extra-messy game of Splatterjack and there is music from Becky Hill.

programme.title: Top Class
programme.desc: Two teams take on the challenge of answering questions

programme.title: Top Class
programme.desc: Two teams take on the challenge of answering questions

programme.title: My Life
programme.desc: Documentary following two sisters as they join their father on a business trip to Antarctica, where they film a colony of penguins and learn to live in the extreme cold

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Danger Mouse's addiction to challenges threatens the entire world

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: The secret agent battles an army of vengeful soft toys. Revival of the classic cartoon about the adventures of a crimebusting rodent and his sidekick Penfold. With the voices of Alexander Armstrong and Kevin Eldon

programme.title: Wallace & Gromit: A Close Shave
programme.desc: Nick Park's Oscar-winning animated characters embark on their third adventure. Wallace falls in love with the proprietor of the local wool shop - only for Gromit to be framed for sheep rustling. With the voices of Peter Sallis and Anne Reid

programme.title: How to Train Your Dragon: Legend of the Boneknapper Dragon
programme.desc: Animated short film featuring the characters from How to Train Your Dragon. Gobber's house is burned down, and he becomes convinced a legendary monster is responsible. He sets off on a quest to slay the beast, but none of his friends believe it even exists. With the voices of Craig Ferguson, Jay Baruchel and Gerard Butler

programme.title: Heirs of the Night
programme.desc: Dracula and the vampires begin to realise that Alisa has enormous powers. Excited about her new powers Alisa tries them out, much to the amusement of Lars and Ivy.

programme.title: Secret Life of Boys
programme.desc: Granny Bob arrives on her motorbike, declaring she will whip the kids into shape

programme.title: So Awkward
programme.desc: Martha feels neglected by her workaholic mother, so Jas helps her express her inner rebel in a bid to get noticed. However, the plan backfires, and Jas finds herself in detention

programme.desc: Piper's attempts to come clean to Amy fall flat after she learns some unexpected news

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: Muffy and Francine are convinced that Binky cheated on a test.

programme.title: Shaun the Sheep
programme.desc: The Farmer discovers he has no fresh milk for his morning cup of tea, and sets off in great haste to find some - only to become ensnared in a rush-hour traffic jam

programme.title: Danger Mouse

programme.desc: Boy's nose makes a weird whistling sound when he is asleep, which is no big deal until it is captured on video and goes viral - but is this new-found popularity a good thing?

programme.title: Grandpa's Great Escape
programme.desc: Jack's grandfather was a Second World War flying ace who now sadly has Alzheimer's disease, and when his family can no longer look after him he is moved to Twilight Towers care home, run by matron Miss Dandy. It soon becomes clear she is running the place for her own ulterior motives and it is up to Jack to help his grandpa make a daring escape and once again take to the skies in a Spitfire. Adaptation of David Walliams' children's book, starring Tom Courtenay, Kit Connor and Jennifer Saunders

programme.title: Max and Harvey: FOMO
programme.desc: Max and Harvey bring you up to speed with all things CBBC and more! Packed full of vlogs and funny challenges

programme.title: Horrible Histories
programme.desc: A selection of monarchs, emperors and political leaders from the series, including naughty Napoleon annoying his Josephine, and horrid Henry VIII getting married - again. Plus, a song from wily Winston Churchill. Starring Rowan Atkinson, Kevin Eldon, Ben Miller and Tom Rosenthal

programme.title: Danny & Mick
programme.desc: A small stain leads to big problems as Danny and Mick try to paper over their mistake

programme.title: The Deep

Love Monster and Good Idea Puppy go together and have a great time, but Bad Idea Puppy has to go on his own and struggles to slide down. After the slide, they visit the Frosty Frolics Fayre, and Good Idea Puppy and Love Monster accidentally choose activities made for two all day long. Finally, just as Love Monster and Good Idea Puppy are about to go on a tandem ski through the forest, Bad Idea Puppy asks if this time he can go with his cousin so he won't be the odd puppy out. programme.desc: Rory meets Sophia whose cat, Oscar, is getting a check-up at the vets before trying a new treatment, while Ferne visits a zoo vet who is checking on their arctic fox

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: Four-year-old Ottalie lives on a farm, and heads out with her father to count and feed the sheep. It's a foggy day and the sheep are hard to find but they soon appear when they know it's food time

programme.desc: Maddie learns how a lamp works and visits a factory to see how a playhouse is made.

programme.desc: Gem and her teams of Swashbucklers try to win her jewels back from pirates Cook, Line and Sinker. Game show for children
