programme.title: Mimi and the Mountain Dragon
programme.desc: Animated adaptation of Michael Morpurgo's children's book about a brave but shy girl from a village that lives in constant fear of a terrible mountain dragon. On a snowy winter's night, she finds a baby dragon asleep in her family's woodshed, and sets out on a terrible journey through a blizzard to return the infant to its dreaded mother. Featuring an introduction by the author and a score by Oscar-winning composer Rachel Portman, performed by the BBC Philharmonic

programme.title: The Penguins of Madagascar
programme.desc: Skipper, Kowalski, Private and Rico embark on an adventure on Christmas Eve. Animated film spin-off of the popular films, featuring the voices of John DiMaggio and Tom McGrath

programme.title: Shaun the Sheep Movie (2015)
programme.desc: The lovable lamb's plans for a fun day off turn into something far more exciting when he's forced to take a trip to the big city to save the farmer, who has lost his memory. Can Shaun and his friends return home safely following their adventure? Animation, featuring the voices of Justin Fletcher, John Sparkes and Omid Djalili

programme.title: Wallace & Gromit in The Curse of the Were-Rabbit (2005)
programme.desc: Inventor Wallace and his canine sidekick Gromit have set up a pest-control service, and are called in to stop rabbits overrunning a garden show. However, the pair are overwhelmed when a destructive giant bunny arrives on the scene, while an interfering aristocrat contrives to make matters worse. Nick Park's Oscar-winning animated adventure, with the voices of Peter Sallis, Ralph Fiennes and Helena Bonham Carter

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Choir director and vocal coach Mark De-Lisser joins Soul singer Mica Paris as she shares her journey of faith, and speaks about the strength of their family unit. Featuring favourite hymns and three Gospel performances from Mica herself

programme.title: Monsters University (2013)
programme.desc: Student creatures Mike Wazowski and James P Sullivan, aka Sulley, attend university to learn how to be scary, but their rivalry gets them both kicked off the course. The monstrous duo must learn how to work together if they are to realise their dreams of terrifying children. Winning the Scare Games will make the difference between Mike staying at the campus or having to leave. Pixar's animated comedy prequel to Monsters Inc, with the voices of John Goodman, Billy Crystal, Steve Buscemi and Helen Mirren

programme.title: Book of Dragons (2011)
programme.desc: Animated short based on the 2010 fantasy film How to Train Your Dragon. Hiccup, Astrid, Fishlegs, Toothless and Gobber uncover the legend behind the Book of Dragons and reveal insider training secrets regarding some new, never-before-seen mythical creatures. With the voices of Jay Baruchel and Craig Ferguson

programme.title: The Sound of Music (1965)
programme.desc: A cheerful novice nun is employed as governess to the seven children of an austere widower. As she brings light, laughter and a love of all things harmonious to a family previously beset by grief and strict discipline she also falls in love with her employer. But before long, the looming threat of the Second World War jeopardises their happy household. Oscar-winning Rodgers and Hammerstein musical, starring Julie Andrews, Christopher Plummer and Eleanor Parker

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison meets Simon King in Bristol to get a crash course in how to photograph wildlife. They attempt to capture on camera birds in flight over the Avon Gorge, inquisitive city squirrels, and a herd of deer who live semi-wild in the nearby Ashton Court Estate. Plus, a look through Countryfile's archive for some of Ellie's wildlife highlights

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce revisits some of the show's most exciting finds, and discovers what happened when the cameras stopped rolling. She reveals the outcome of the auction for a special guitar belonging to the Beatles and the mystery of the missing blueprint for the Royal Albert Hall, before going behind the scenes of Pinewood Film and TV Studios to learn about its wartime history, its famous costumes, and a giant golden gong

programme.title: Black Narcissus
programme.desc: Sister Clodagh and her group of Anglo-Catholic nuns travel from Darjeeling on a mission to the Palace of Mopu in the Himalayas. The order starts to repair the palace and opens a school. However, the unsettling atmosphere of their surroundings reawakens disturbing memories in Clodagh. Adaptation of Rumer Godden's novel, starring Gemma Arterton

programme.title: Give My Head Peace
programme.desc: The reading of Big Mervyn's will proves the event of the year in the Knee Breakers, and naturally there's a big surprise in store

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the day's Premier League action, including Liverpool v West Bromwich Albion at Anfield and Wolverhampton Wanderers v Tottenham Hotspur at Molineux. Although they have not been as dominant as last term, the Reds are chasing a second successive league championship and will have expected to prevail against the Baggies, while Spurs are also chasing the title and faced a real test of their credentials against Wolves. Plus, Leeds United v Burnley, West Ham United v Brighton & Hove Albion and a round-up of yesterday's goals

programme.title: Spider-Man: Homecoming (2017)
programme.desc: Peter Parker balances life as an ordinary high-school student with his superhero alter-ego Spider-Man, while waiting in vain to join his mentor Iron Man on a world-saving mission. Determined to prove he is ready for bigger challenges, he investigates a spree of crimes committed with advanced weapons - leading him into a battle with a ruthless winged enemy on the hunt for priceless technology. Superhero adventure, starring Tom Holland, Michael Keaton and Robert Downey Jr

programme.title: West Is West (2010)
programme.desc: A traditionally minded Muslim father living in 1970s Salford decides the solution to his troubled youngest son's problems is to reconnect with his roots. He takes the reluctant teenager on a trip to Pakistan, only to have an awkward encounter with his first wife. Comedy sequel to East Is East, with Om Puri, Aqib Khan and Linda Bassett

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Jon Kay and Nina Warhurst present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Planes (2013)
programme.desc: A crop duster longs to be a racing plane, but is hamstrung by a fear of heights. With the aid of a veteran flyer, he learns to conquer his phobia and compete against some of the world's greatest flyers in a round-the-world race. Animated adventure spin-off from Pixar's Cars, with the voices of Dane Cook, Teri Hatcher and Stacy Keach

programme.title: Paddington 2 (2017)
programme.desc: The lovable little bear has settled into his new life with the Brown family in London, becoming a much-loved member of the local community - until his search for a birthday present for his aunt leads to him being framed as a thief. While the Browns try to clear Paddington's name, he tries to make himself at home in prison, winning over the other convicts and getting involved in an escape attempt. Comedy adventure, starring Hugh Bonneville, Hugh Grant and Sally Hawkins, with Ben Whishaw providing the voice of Paddington

programme.title: Revolting Rhymes
programme.desc: The first of two collections of animations based on poems from Roald Dahl's children's book, featuring the author's twists on Snow White & the Seven Dwarves, Little Red Riding Hood and The Three Little Pigs. Best friends Snow and Red take on a villainous queen with a magic mirror, before Red faces another challenge when two hungry wolves are on the prowl - one disguised as her own grandmother, while the other has his eye on a trio of helpless pigs. With the voices of Tamsin Greig, Dominic West, Rob Brydon, Bertie Carvel, Gemma Chan and Rose Leslie

programme.title: Revolting Rhymes
programme.desc: Conclusion. Two more animations based on tales from Roald Dahl's children's book, which puts the author's comic spin on the stories of Cinderella and Jack and the Beanstalk. Jack is madly in love with his next door neighbour Cindy - but after attending a ball, she only has eyes for a vain prince. He finds himself distracted from the pursuit of true love when a giant beanstalk starts growing at the bottom of his garden. The star-studded voice cast includes David Walliams and Rob Brydon as the ugly sisters

programme.title: Inside Out (2015)
programme.desc: Inside 11-year-old Riley's head lives a team of five that controls her emotions. Led by the eternally upbeat Joy, they have always ensured she lived a happy life - but when Riley's parents move to a new city, she is plunged into depression and the emotions face a crisis that threatens their entire world. Pixar animated fantasy, with the voices of Amy Poehler and Phyllis Smith

programme.title: How to Train Your Dragon: Legend of the Boneknapper Dragon
programme.desc: Animated short film featuring the characters from How to Train Your Dragon. Gobber's house is burned down, and he becomes convinced a legendary monster is responsible. He sets off on a quest to slay the beast, but none of his friends believe it even exists. With the voices of Craig Ferguson, Jay Baruchel and Gerard Butler

programme.title: The Jungle Book (2016)
programme.desc: A boy is raised by animals in the jungles of India, but his carefree life is threatened by the return of a fierce tiger with a grudge against humans. The youngster faces a difficult choice between remaining with his friends or returning to his own people. Live action remake of Disney's animated adventure based on Rudyard Kipling's stories, starring Neel Sethi, with the voices of Bill Murray, Ben Kingsley and Idris Elba

programme.title: Wallace & Gromit: A Matter of Loaf and Death
programme.desc: The duo open their own bakery, Top Bun, and business is soon booming - not least because a 'cereal killer' is targeting all the rival bakers in town. Even better, Wallace has caught the eye of local beauty Piella Bakewell. But Gromit is more concerned that his master might become the killer's next victim. With the voices of Peter Sallis and Sally Lindsay

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present as famous faces take part in a festive edition of the general knowledge quiz, in which they try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. Taking part are Slade guitarist Dave Hill, Mud's Rob Davis, actress Sally Lindsay, poet and broadcaster Roger McGough, and singers Sonia, Jamelia, Keren Woodward and Sara Dallin

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: Former winners Miles Jupp, Zoe Lyons, Neil Hannon and Lucy Porter are put to the test by John Humphrys in a special champion of champions edition of the quiz, answering questions on the specialist subjects of David Gower, Jacques Cousteau, Merchant Ivory's EM Forster film adaptations and Victoria Wood

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games Night
programme.desc: Sarah Hadland, Alex Horne, Craig Revel Horwood and Charlene White compete in a festive edition of the general knowledge quiz, taking on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host. A winner will be declared following a quick-fire round at the end of the show, and the victor takes home a prize and the House of Games Christmas trophy

programme.title: Dynasties
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates the story of young meerkat queen Maghogho on her quest to establish a clan in one of the harshest places on earth. The territory on the Makgadikgadi salt pans in Botswana is at the limit of what meerkats can endure and yet it is here that she must raise her pups in the face of rivals, predators and immense dust storms

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Max puts pressure on Linda as things between them start to become very real. Martin tells Ruby he wishes she could bury the hatchet with Stacey, while Shirley is disappointed that Mick still refuses to engage with her. Denise opens up about her fears that Lucas will hurt her - so Kim comes up with a plan

programme.title: Black Narcissus
programme.desc: Sister Ruth's health worsens as winter sets in and the ghost of Srimati plagues her as Angu Ayah tells her more of the princess's tragic fate. Dilip Rai's arrival in search of education presents Sister Clodagh with a dilemma and she is forced to turn to Mr Dean for help, and as she struggles to keep control of the convent, Sister Philippa announces her desire to leave. Forbidden feelings between Clodagh, Sister Ruth and Mr Dean come to a head at Christmas, interrupted by the dramatic arrival of Sister Adela and Fr Roberts

programme.title: Gloria: My Life on TV
programme.desc: Gloria Hunniford looks back at her life and career, revealing what it has been like to be woman, mother and grandmother on TV and radio for more than half a century. She talks about her experience as a young woman working in the male-dominated newsrooms of the era, and how she survived personal tragedies such as the loss of her daughter Caron Keating to cancer in 2004

programme.title: Mission: Impossible (1996)
programme.desc: Secret agents suspect there is a traitor in their organisation when their latest assignment ends in disaster. The sole survivor is accused of being the mole, forcing him to go on the run to clear his name. However, in the process, he discovers a former Russian spy is selling information about the world's leading undercover operatives. Action thriller based on the 1960s TV series, starring Tom Cruise, Jon Voight and Ving Rhames

programme.title: Man Like Mobeen
programme.desc: While trying to do a good deed by helping out at the local food bank, Mobeen is also under strict orders from Uncle Khan to launder money - and juggling the two things could leave him with a deadly debt that's impossible to pay

programme.title: Wild on Water
programme.desc: Karen Patterson joins Jen Firth at low tide to discover why Strangford Lough, County Down supports such a diverse range of wildlife, especially the birds

programme.title: Mindhorn (2016)
programme.desc: The star of a 1980s sci-fi cop show sets out to make it in Hollywood, but ends up with his life and career in ruins. Years later, he gets the chance to be a real hero when the police need his help. A dangerous fugitive is on the loose, who believes the character the actor played - a detective with a bionic eye that can detect lies - is real. Comedy, starring Julian Barratt, Essie Davis and Steve Coogan

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Jon Kay and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: The NeverEnding Story (1984)
programme.desc: A lonely boy, neglected by his father and bullied at school, finds comfort in a mysterious book about a young hero's quest to save his world from destruction. However, as he reads the tale, he begins to become part of the story. Fantasy adventure based on Michael Edne's novel, starring Barret Oliver, Noah Hathaway, Gerald McRaney, Moses Gunn and Patricia Hayes

programme.title: The Croods (2013)
programme.desc: Prehistoric family the Croods find themselves with nowhere to live when their cave is destroyed. They embark on a dangerous trek through an unstable landscape overrun with all manner of dangerous predators in search of a new home, but their domestic squabbles prove as much of an obstacle as the wildlife. Animated comedy, with the voices of Nicolas Cage and Ryan Reynolds

programme.title: Mr Stink (2012)
programme.desc: Family comedy based on the children's novel by David Walliams. Twelve-year-old Chloe leads a lonely existence - her mother is too busy with her political ambitions to take any interest in her daughter, her hen-pecked dad is hiding a secret of his own and she cannot hope to compete with her 'perfect' younger sister - to say nothing of the nasty girls who make her school life a misery. So when she meets a tramp in need of somewhere to stay, she invites him to hide in her family's garden shed - but it soon turns out there is more to her smelly guest than meets the eye. Downton Abbey's Hugh Bonneville stars, with Sheridan Smith, Johnny Vegas and Isabella Blake-Thomas, and an appearance from Britain's Got Talent winner Pudsey the dog

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: The Farmer's Llamas
programme.desc: When the Farmer and Bitzer go to a country fair, Shaun the Sheep steals away with them intent on causing mischief. He spies an auction where he sees three exotic and very crafty llamas going under the hammer. Instantly impressed by their amusing pranks, Shaun sneakily gets the Farmer to buy them, but it's not long before these cheeky newcomers wreak havoc. Animated fun, with the voice of Justin Fletcher

programme.title: Zootropolis (2016)
programme.desc: In a city populated by animals, a rabbit achieves her dream of becoming the first bunny to become a police officer - but faces prejudice at every turn. When the disappearance of an otter hints at a terrible conspiracy in the city, she enlists the aid of a con-artist fox in solving the mystery. Disney animated comedy, with the voices of Ginnifer Goodwin, Jason Bateman and Idris Elba

programme.title: Frozen (2013)
programme.desc: A princess with the power to freeze anything she touches loses control of her abilities and plunges the kingdom into a perpetual winter. She goes into hiding, but her sister remains determined that she can be saved from her own magic and sets off on a quest to find her, aided by a mountain man and a living snowman. Disney animated fantasy adventure, with the voices of Kristen Bell and Idina Menzel

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present another edition of their unorthodox, star-filled quiz show. This time, all the contestants are from the world of music, and as befits the time of year, the entry requirement is that they must have released a successful Christmas hit. Stop the Cavalry singer Jona Lewie teams up with Mike Batt, who released Wombling Merry Christmas, while 1972 Christmas Number One hit-maker Jimmy Osmond pairs with his 2015 counterpart - X Factor's Ben Haenow. Choirmaster Gareth Malone joins forces with Samantha Stevenson of the Military Wives Choir, and Justin and Dan Hawkins of The Darkness also compete, before playing out the show with their festive favourite Christmas Time (Don't Let the Bells End)

programme.title: Penguins: Meet the Family
programme.desc: Documentary following penguins living in unexpected environments, including satellite imagery of previously undiscovered colonies of flightless birds. Among their habitats are dense forests, desert burrows and city streets. The programme reveals the challenges they face in making their homes in these challenging environments, from evading predators to scaling rocks, and captures the lifelong bonds formed between family groups

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Patrick is desperate to help Denise and takes matters into his own hands - although he may have only made matters worse. Ruby warns Stacey to stay out of her marriage, while Jay makes out that the kiss with Honey was a mistake - is he hiding his true feelings?

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The first of two special Christmas episodes of the culinary challenge, in which eight finalists and semi-finalists from past years return to face a festive test of their skills. The first episode features Danny Parker from the 2014 series, 2017's Louisa Ellis, 2009's Marianne Lumb, and 2018 semi-finalist William Chilila. Monica Galetti sets them the task of making festive canapés, before they move on to the dreaded skills test and a seasonally themed two-course menu

programme.title: Black Narcissus
programme.desc: Sister Adela and Father Roberts prove unlikely allies for Ruth, forcing Clodagh to impose strict order or risk losing control of the convent, as well as herself. But events escalate as Dilip Rai and Kanchi are discovered together, with dramatic consequences. Ruth flees the palace to declare her love to Mr Dean, who is defending the convent against retaliation from the village. Conclusion of the adaptation of Rumer Godden's novel, starring Gemma Arterton and Alessandro Nivola

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League fixtures, including Manchester United v Wolverhampton Wanderers at Old Trafford and Brighton & Hove Albion v Arsenal at Amex Stadium. United and Wolves met on four occasions in all competitions last term, with three of those contests ending level and the Red Devils prevailing 1-0 at home in an FA Cup replay in the other. Brighton did the double over the Gunners last season, winning 2-1 home and away, and will be hopeful of shocking the Londoners once again. Plus, a round-up of the rest of tonight and yesterday's fixtures

programme.title: Point Break (1991)
programme.desc: An FBI agent infiltrates a Californian surfing gang suspected of carrying out a series of armed robberies wearing masks modelled on former US presidents. However, his loyalties are tested to the limits when he is befriended by the gang's leader. Kathryn Bigelow's thriller, starring Keanu Reeves, Patrick Swayze, Gary Busey and Lori Petty

programme.title: This Country
programme.desc: The annual harvest festival brings the whole village together, but one resident's announcement causes unexpected emotional consequences for the community. Spoof documentary exploring modern life in the Cotswolds, written by and starring siblings Daisy May and Charlie Cooper

programme.title: Anita & Me (2002)
programme.desc: Twelve-year-old Meena strikes up an unlikely friendship with blonde teenage tearaway Anita. Despite their ethnic and social differences, the girls manage to support each other through difficult times while their families cope with life's problems. Comedy drama set in a Midlands village in the 1970s and based on the novel by Meera Syal, starring Chandeep Uppal, Anna Brewster, Max Beesley, Sanjeev Bhaskar, Kathy Burke, Lynn Redgrave and Ayesha Dharker

programme.title: The Reith Lectures
programme.desc: In his final lecture, Mark Carney turns his attention to climate change, arguing that the roots of our environmental emergency lie in a deeper crisis of values

programme.title: Scottish Parliament
programme.desc: The statement in the Scottish Parliament by the First Minister, Nicola Sturgeon, on the Covid-19 pandemic and the possible need to strengthen the top level of Covid-19 restrictions in Scotland as the whole of the Scottish mainland moves to level 4 from Boxing Day, from Tuesday 22 December

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: Interviews with newsmakers and personalities from across the globe

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Coverage of the joint session of the Science and Technology committee and the Health and Social Care committee looking at the performance of NHS Test and Trace with evidence from the Executive Chair, Dido Harding, and its Chief Medical Adviser, Dr Susan Hopkins.

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: BBC Parliament's programme looking back at the final full week in Westminster of 2020. First shown on BBC Parliament on Thursday 17 December

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: What is the prospect for reconciliation for Northern Ireland's two communities - Unionist and Nationalist? Stephen Sackur speaks to Billy Hutchinson, advocate of co-existence

programme.title: Prime Minister's Questions
programme.desc: Coverage of questions in the House of Commons to Prime Minister Boris Johnson

programme.title: Briefings
programme.desc: The news conference held in Downing Street by the Prime Minister, Boris Johnson, on the announcement of a Tier 4 for London and many areas of the South East as well as changes to the restrictions over Christmas, alongside the government's chief scientific adviser, Sir Patrick Vallance, and the Chief Medical Officer for England, Professor Chris Whitty, from Saturday 19 December.

programme.title: Briefings
programme.desc: Labour leader Sir Keir Starmer giving a speech on devolution across the UK and moving power out of London, from Monday 21 December

programme.title: Briefings
programme.desc: Director of the Victoria and Albert Museum, Tristram Hunt, delivering this year's Orwell Memorial Lecture on 'Decolonising The Wonder House: Orwell, Empire and the Museum', from Monday 16 November.

programme.title: House of Commons
programme.desc: The statement to the House of Commons by the Chancellor of the Exchequer, Rishi Sunak, on this year's spending review and the latest Office for Budget Responsibility reports with the responses from Shadow Chancellor Anneliese Dodds and SNP Treasury spokesperson, Alison Thewliss, from earlier today

programme.title: Lords Questions
programme.desc: Coverage of the last question session of 2020 in the House of Lords

programme.title: The Reith Lectures
programme.desc: Former Governor of the Bank of England Mark Carney charts how financial value has come to be held in higher esteem than human value, beginning by assessing what value actually means. How looks at whether the way it is assessed both shapes human values and constrains choices, and looks at how the markets affect society. Introduced by Anita Anand

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: The Digital, Culture, Media and Sport select committee session on the economics of music streaming with evidence from Colin Young, Tom Gray, Tom Frederikse, Guy Garvey, Ed O'Brien, Nadine Shah, from Tuesday 24 November.

programme.title: Scottish First Minister's Questions
programme.desc: Recorded coverage of questions to First Minister Nicola Sturgeon in the Scottish Parliament

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: Stephen Sackur speaks to Lt General HR McMaster who served as Mr Trump's national security adviser until he was fired in 2018. Will the Trump years be seen as an aberration or a marker of change?

programme.title: Westminster Hall
programme.desc: Coverage of a debate on the future of football in Westminster Hall, from Wednesday 25 November.

programme.title: Northern Ireland Assembly
programme.desc: Live coverage of questions in the Northern Ireland Assembly

programme.title: Briefings
programme.desc: Dr Chris Whitty, the Chief Medical Officer for England, delivering a lecture at Gresham College on 'the Geography of Ill Health', from Wednesday 25 November.

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Joint session of the Health and Social Care and Science and Technology committees. MPs will hear from Prof Devi Sridhar, Professor of Global Public Health at the University of Edinburgh, and Health and Social Care Secretary Matt Hancock, from Tuesday 24 November.

programme.title: The Reith Lectures
programme.desc: In his second Reith Lecture, Mark Carney, the former Governor of the Bank of England, takes his audience back to the high drama of the financial crisis of 2008, which ended a period when bankers saw themselves as unassailable Masters of the Universe. More than a decade on, how much have the bankers changed their ways? How far has the financial sector changed? Dr Carney says that society must remain vigilant and resist the 'three lies of finance'. If not, it will live with a system that is ill-prepared for the next crisis. Presented by Anita Anand

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: Stephen Sackur speaks to Jacob Bleacher, chief exploration scientist at Nasa

programme.title: Welsh First Minister's Questions
programme.desc: Recorded coverage from the Welsh Assembly as First Minister Mark Drakeford tackles queries on issues affecting life in Wales

programme.title: House of Lords
programme.desc: The statement in the House of Lords on the Integrated Review of Security, Defence, Development and Foreign Policy, from earlier today

programme.title: House of Commons
programme.desc: Debate from the chamber

programme.title: House of Commons
programme.desc: Debate from the chamber

programme.title: America This Week
programme.desc: C-SPAN's coverage of the Senate Judiciary committee hearing on the role of tech giants during the presidential election campaign with Facebook CEO Mark Zuckerberg and Twitter CEO Jack Dorsey, from Tuesday 17 November.

programme.title: Westminster Hall
programme.desc: Westminster Hall - Funding for Transport for London Coverage of a debate in Westminster Hall on funding for Transport for London, from Monday 23 November

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: In a special edition of the programme, HARDtalk is in northwest England, to the area known as the Lake District. The landscape is beautiful but is not wild. The fields have been shaped by generations of shepherds and stockmen. Stephen Sackur speaks to James Rebanks whose farm has been in his family's hands for at least 600 years. He has become a nationally renowned advocate for a better kind of farming that is more sustainable and environmentally responsible. But are his ideas compatible with putting affordable food on all of our tables?

programme.title: Northern Ireland Assembly
programme.desc: Live coverage of questions to the Executive Office in the Northern Ireland Assembly

programme.title: The Reith Lectures
programme.desc: Mark Carney, the former Governor of the Bank of England, observes that the pandemic has forced states to confront how people value health, wealth and opportunity. During the first few months of the crisis, most states chose to value human life more than the economic well-being of the nation-state. But if that seems to be changing how do we assess value in this sense? Dr Carney elucidates surprising differences in the financial value put on a human life in different nations - and goes on to argue that this reductionist approach fails to take into account deeper thinking about the worth of human existence. Presented by Anita Anand

programme.title: House of Commons
programme.desc: The short debate in the House of Commons marking the 60th anniversary of Coronation Street, opened by the MP and former Coronation Street actor, Tracy Brabin, from Wednesday 2 December

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Live coverage of the Science and Technology select committee session on UK Science, Research and Technology Capability and Influence in Global Disease Outbreaks focusing on the new strain of Covid-19 in the UK with evidence from Professor Peter Horby, Chair of the New and Emerging Respiratory Virus Threats Advisory Group (NERVTAG), Professor Wendy Barclay, Head of the Department of Infectious Disease, Imperial College London and Professor Neil Ferguson OBE, Director at MRC Centre for Global Infectious Disease Analysis, Imperial College London

programme.title: Senedd Cymru - Welsh Parliament
programme.desc: Conservative-led debate in the Welsh Parliament on the impact of Covid on health services, from Wednesday 25 November. First shown on BBC Parliament on Saturday 28 November

programme.title: House of Commons
programme.desc: Live coverage of the day's proceedings in the House of Commons including backbench business debates on the regulation and prevention of online harms and on International Men's Day followed by an adjournment debate on the Department for Health and Social Care's Independent Reconfiguration Panel.

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: As soon as he emerged from his coma Alexei Navalny, the Russian opposition leader apparently poisoned by novichok nerve agent, expressed his determination to return to Moscow. But what future is there for an anti-Putin political movement in a country where dissent is all too often seriously bad for your health? Stephen Sackur speaks to Leonid Volkov, opposition politician and chief of staff to Mr Navalny. Is there any weakening of the Kremlin's grip on power?

programme.title: House of Lords
programme.desc: Peers quiz government minister Lord Parkinson on plans for an additional bank holiday to mark the Queen's Platinum Jubilee in 2022, from Monday 16 November

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: The Northern Ireland Affairs select committee session on cross-border cooperation on policing, security and criminal justice with evidence from the Police Service of Northern Ireland, the National Crime Agency and Her Majesty's Customs and Revenue, from Wednesday 4 November.

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: What is the point of the world's nuclear watchdog the International Atomic Energy Agency? Its task is to ensure that countries intent on developing nuclear power don't use their programmes as cover for development of weapons of mass destruction. But is the task impossible? HARDtalk's Stephen Sackur speaks to the new IAEA chief, Rafael Grossi. From the continued bitter arguments over Iran, to North Korea, and Saudi Arabia, is the IAEA another example of a global agency undermined by geopolitical division?

programme.title: House of Commons
programme.desc: Live coverage of questions in the House of Commons to the International Trade Secretary Liz Truss and her team of ministers.

programme.title: The Reith Lectures
programme.desc: In his final lecture, Mark Carney turns his attention to climate change, arguing that the roots of our environmental emergency lie in a deeper crisis of values

programme.title: Scottish Parliament
programme.desc: The statement in the Scottish Parliament by the First Minister, Nicola Sturgeon, on the Covid-19 pandemic and the possible need to strengthen the top level of Covid-19 restrictions in Scotland as the whole of the Scottish mainland moves to level 4 from Boxing Day, from Tuesday 22 December

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: Interviews with newsmakers and personalities from across the globe

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Coverage of the joint session of the Science and Technology committee and the Health and Social Care committee looking at the performance of NHS Test and Trace with evidence from the Executive Chair, Dido Harding, and its Chief Medical Adviser, Dr Susan Hopkins.

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: BBC Parliament's programme looking back at the final full week in Westminster of 2020. First shown on BBC Parliament on Thursday 17 December

programme.title: Prime Minister's Questions
programme.desc: Coverage of questions in the House of Commons to Prime Minister Boris Johnson

programme.title: Briefings
programme.desc: The news conference held in Downing Street by the Prime Minister, Boris Johnson, on the announcement of a Tier 4 for London and many areas of the South East as well as changes to the restrictions over Christmas, alongside the government's chief scientific adviser, Sir Patrick Vallance, and the Chief Medical Officer for England, Professor Chris Whitty, from Saturday 19 December.

programme.title: Briefings
programme.desc: Labour leader Sir Keir Starmer giving a speech on devolution across the UK and moving power out of London, from Monday 21 December

programme.title: Briefings
programme.desc: Director of the Victoria and Albert Museum, Tristram Hunt, delivering this year's Orwell Memorial Lecture on 'Decolonising The Wonder House: Orwell, Empire and the Museum', from Monday 16 November.

programme.title: House of Commons
programme.desc: The statement to the House of Commons by the Chancellor of the Exchequer, Rishi Sunak, on this year's spending review and the latest Office for Budget Responsibility reports with the responses from Shadow Chancellor Anneliese Dodds and SNP Treasury spokesperson, Alison Thewliss, from earlier today

programme.title: House of Commons
programme.desc: The statement to the House of Commons by the Chancellor of the Exchequer, Rishi Sunak, on this year's spending review and the latest Office for Budget Responsibility reports with the responses from Shadow Chancellor Anneliese Dodds and SNP Treasury spokesperson, Alison Thewliss, from earlier today

programme.title: Lords Questions
programme.desc: Coverage of the last question session of 2020 in the House of Lords

programme.title: The Reith Lectures
programme.desc: Former Governor of the Bank of England Mark Carney charts how financial value has come to be held in higher esteem than human value, beginning by assessing what value actually means. How looks at whether the way it is assessed both shapes human values and constrains choices, and looks at how the markets affect society. Introduced by Anita Anand

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: The Digital, Culture, Media and Sport select committee session on the economics of music streaming with evidence from Colin Young, Tom Gray, Tom Frederikse, Guy Garvey, Ed O'Brien, Nadine Shah, from Tuesday 24 November.

programme.title: Scottish First Minister's Questions
programme.desc: Recorded coverage of questions to First Minister Nicola Sturgeon in the Scottish Parliament

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: Stephen Sackur speaks to Lt General HR McMaster who served as Mr Trump's national security adviser until he was fired in 2018. Will the Trump years be seen as an aberration or a marker of change?

programme.title: Westminster Hall
programme.desc: Coverage of a debate on the future of football in Westminster Hall, from Wednesday 25 November.

programme.title: Northern Ireland Assembly
programme.desc: Live coverage of questions in the Northern Ireland Assembly

programme.title: Briefings
programme.desc: Dr Chris Whitty, the Chief Medical Officer for England, delivering a lecture at Gresham College on 'the Geography of Ill Health', from Wednesday 25 November.

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Joint session of the Health and Social Care and Science and Technology committees. MPs will hear from Prof Devi Sridhar, Professor of Global Public Health at the University of Edinburgh, and Health and Social Care Secretary Matt Hancock, from Tuesday 24 November.

programme.title: The Reith Lectures
programme.desc: In his second Reith Lecture, Mark Carney, the former Governor of the Bank of England, takes his audience back to the high drama of the financial crisis of 2008, which ended a period when bankers saw themselves as unassailable Masters of the Universe. More than a decade on, how much have the bankers changed their ways? How far has the financial sector changed? Dr Carney says that society must remain vigilant and resist the 'three lies of finance'. If not, it will live with a system that is ill-prepared for the next crisis. Presented by Anita Anand

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: Stephen Sackur speaks to Jacob Bleacher, chief exploration scientist at Nasa

programme.title: Welsh First Minister's Questions
programme.desc: Recorded coverage from the Welsh Assembly as First Minister Mark Drakeford tackles queries on issues affecting life in Wales

programme.title: House of Lords
programme.desc: The statement in the House of Lords on the Integrated Review of Security, Defence, Development and Foreign Policy, from earlier today

programme.title: House of Commons
programme.desc: Debate from the chamber

programme.title: America This Week
programme.desc: C-SPAN's coverage of the Senate Judiciary committee hearing on the role of tech giants during the presidential election campaign with Facebook CEO Mark Zuckerberg and Twitter CEO Jack Dorsey, from Tuesday 17 November.

programme.title: Westminster Hall
programme.desc: Westminster Hall - Funding for Transport for London Coverage of a debate in Westminster Hall on funding for Transport for London, from Monday 23 November

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: In a special edition of the programme, HARDtalk is in northwest England, to the area known as the Lake District. The landscape is beautiful but is not wild. The fields have been shaped by generations of shepherds and stockmen. Stephen Sackur speaks to James Rebanks whose farm has been in his family's hands for at least 600 years. He has become a nationally renowned advocate for a better kind of farming that is more sustainable and environmentally responsible. But are his ideas compatible with putting affordable food on all of our tables?

programme.title: Northern Ireland Assembly
programme.desc: Live coverage of questions to the Executive Office in the Northern Ireland Assembly

programme.title: The Reith Lectures
programme.desc: Mark Carney, the former Governor of the Bank of England, observes that the pandemic has forced states to confront how people value health, wealth and opportunity. During the first few months of the crisis, most states chose to value human life more than the economic well-being of the nation-state. But if that seems to be changing how do we assess value in this sense? Dr Carney elucidates surprising differences in the financial value put on a human life in different nations - and goes on to argue that this reductionist approach fails to take into account deeper thinking about the worth of human existence. Presented by Anita Anand

programme.title: House of Commons
programme.desc: The short debate in the House of Commons marking the 60th anniversary of Coronation Street, opened by the MP and former Coronation Street actor, Tracy Brabin, from Wednesday 2 December

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Live coverage of the Science and Technology select committee session on UK Science, Research and Technology Capability and Influence in Global Disease Outbreaks focusing on the new strain of Covid-19 in the UK with evidence from Professor Peter Horby, Chair of the New and Emerging Respiratory Virus Threats Advisory Group (NERVTAG), Professor Wendy Barclay, Head of the Department of Infectious Disease, Imperial College London and Professor Neil Ferguson OBE, Director at MRC Centre for Global Infectious Disease Analysis, Imperial College London

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: 100 Women
programme.desc: For thousands of years, the Inuit people lived off the land. Nomadic hunters, chasing targets depending on the seasons. Now those weather patterns have utterly and irreversibly shifted. Locals say that sea ice used to form in September. Now, it forms in October or as late as November. The Arctic is now warming at least twice as fast as the rest of the globe, according to the Arctic Program Report Card. Climate change is threatening the Inuits’ survival - both physically and mentally. Inuit women are trying to hold their families together through it all. Life on Thin Ice follows three generations of Inuit women; a grandmother, mother and daughter to see the impact that climate change has had on their community. What else have they lost along with the ice?

programme.title: 100 Women Extra
programme.desc: Finland has a coalition government led by five women - four of them are in their 30s. Their prime minister, Sanna Marin, was the youngest world leader when she was elected. Finland's coalition government has been praised for their quick and decisive action on the coronavirus pandemic, but a coalition can also mean compromise. The BBC has had world-first exclusive access behind the scenes to explore how the coalition works in practice, how representative it is of Finland's society, and what impact the age and sex of the government's main players has had on its policies and effectiveness.

This programme features a selection from that performance originally aired in July 2020. Alongside fans' favourites, the band perform a new Gaelic song and also a beautiful collaboration with Tamzene

programme.title: Twin
programme.desc: Ingrid picks up Karin from the police officer's house and finds her revelations could have major consequences, while Erik discovers Adam's dark secrets. Frank begins to investigate the circumstances that led to the accident and gets a startling confession. In Norwegian

programme.title: Na Tuinichean Mi-thoilichte
programme.desc: Two freshly arrived Scottish settlers in 1780s Nova Scotia are in for a big surprise in their new home and turn to the fast-talking land agent for help

programme.title: Julie Fowlis: Ceol Aig Baile
programme.desc: Filmed during lockdown, Julie Fowlis invites musicians from around the world to perform from their own homes

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: An Gruffalo
programme.desc: A mouse goes on a journey through a deep, dark wood

programme.title: Radan an Rathaid Mhoir (The Highway Rat)
programme.desc: A story about the greedy Highway Rat, a tyrant, scaring all the animals in his path. He's also a thief and a scoundrel, though has an incredibly sweet tooth and it's this that could bring about his downfall

programme.title: Stòiridh
programme.desc: Ceitlin Lilidh Russell Smith reads Rud Beag Gaisgeil, written and illustrated by Nicola Kinnear and translated into Gaelic by Morag Stewart

programme.title: Spaidhc is am Poca-Puist (Spike)
programme.desc: Spike, a worker in Santa Claus's workshop, is given a very important mission - he must try and get all of Santa's letters back safely. With assistance from wise Raymond, can they do it?

programme.title: Geamaichean Gorach
programme.desc: Coinneach Clogaid is trying to win as usual, but things don’t go his way

programme.title: Am Balach anns a' Bhuilgean (Boy in the Bubble)
programme.desc: A boy suffers heartache for the first time and turns to a book of magic for a spell that shields him from emotion, leaving him stuck in a bubble

programme.title: An Là (News)
programme.desc: Local, national and international news and information from BBC ALBA

programme.title: Mòd 2020
programme.desc: Cathy MacDonald is joined by various special guests who recall their own emotions and memories of when they took part in the Royal National Mòd

programme.title: Trusadh (Compelling Stories)
programme.desc: Stories about poaching across the Highlands and Islands of Scotland, told by charismatic local storytellers, including anecdotes of poachers throughout the generations

programme.title: An Taistealaiche/The Far Traveller
programme.desc: Some 500 years before Columbus, the granddaughter of a slave in Scotland crossed the Atlantic eight times. She gave birth to the first European child in North America, and even made a pilgrimage to the Vatican. This documentary retraces Gudrid Thorbjarnardottir's brave steps from Greenland to Newfoundland and beyond

programme.title: Opry an Iúir
programme.desc: Daniel O'Donnell hosts the Irish-language country music show from the Town Hall in Newry, Co Down, featuring Charley Pride

programme.title: Dhan Uisge

programme.title: Alba Today

programme.title: Te Bheag a' Ghruffalo (The Gruffalo's Child)
programme.desc: The Gruffalo's daughter takes a journey through a dark wood in search of the mouse that scared her father, encountering many of his old acquaintances along the way

programme.title: An Fhaochag agus a' Mhuc-mhara
programme.desc: Animated adaptation of the children's book about a tiny snail who longs to see the world and hitches a ride on the tail of a huge humpback whale

programme.title: Stòiridh
programme.desc: Joy Dunlop reads Zoe agus Beans: An Cearcall Draoidheil!, written and illustrated by Chloë and Mick Inkpen and translated into Gaelic by Donald John Macleod

programme.title: Rannan Rapach (Revolting Rhymes)

programme.title: Geamaichean Gorach

programme.title: Lena - An Croitear Og/Lena - The Young Crofter
programme.desc: The life of 10-year-old Lena MacKenzie, from the Isle of Harris, who wants to continue the traditional agricultural way of life that she is being brought up with. The future of the family croft is in safe hands with this passionate young crofter who likes to be at the heart of any crofting work her father has to do

programme.title: An Là (News)
programme.desc: Local, national and international news and information from BBC ALBA

programme.title: Mòd 2020
programme.desc: Cathy MacDonald meets former winners of the Traditional Gold Medal at the Children's Mod

programme.title: Dystonia: Beatha air Fhiaradh
programme.desc: For almost 40 years, DK MacPhee has lived with dystonia, which leaves him unable to straighten his head. DK gives an insight into the impact this has on his daily life and his ongoing battle to find treatment or relief. As his condition has deteriorated, with his head falling further to one side, the more his life has been affected. A number of years ago, he had to give up playing football, weekly, with his friends, something which he very much misses and he is determined to try and play again. Will this be possible?

programme.title: Trusadh (Compelling Stories)

programme.title: Air Iomall-Music from the Edge
programme.desc: Two of Scotland's leading young traditional musicians, Charlie Grey and Joseph Peach, take a musical voyage on the Dutch vessel Wylde Swan to some of the most remote islands off Scotland's north-west coast

programme.title: Fonn mo Bheatha
programme.desc: Cathy Ann MacPhee presents music and chat, with guest Iain Mackay

programme.title: Alba Today

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Fizz is recording chill music to de-stress the world, so he needs quiet. Unluckily, Magoo's come over to borrow a wrench and thinks there's a leak.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Fizz beams himself into Weasel's brain in order to take out Magoo with tranquilliser darts. Magoo loses Mr Cat and enlists his neighbour to find him.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo thinks he's in a triathlon and takes Fizz's superhuman strength headband, which Fizz needs to beat the President at a thumb war

programme.title: Thunderbirds Are Go
programme.desc: John takes a break from his duties aboard Thunderbird 5 to attend a charity event with Lady Penelope - and has to save the day without his Tracy brothers' help when the Hood gatecrashes the party

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: Mike realises there's nothing better to impress your crush with than a little magic trick, but things soon get out of hand.

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: Mike's special date with Iris is interrupted when the raccoons get fleas and head over to Mike's to find something to relieve their itchiness.

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: The raccoons think they will finally be able to steal something from Mike's house when they handcuff Mike's back legs together.

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: It's cold, the boiler is broken, and Mr. Bean has to get warm, fast.

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Bean is cajoled by Irma and spurred by jealousy of the fund-raising star Declan into raising money for a donkey sanctuary.

programme.title: Hotel Transylvania
programme.desc: Mavis' teen crush, Jett Black, visits the hotel

programme.title: LEGO Marvel Avengers: Climate Conundrum
programme.desc: Animated series based on the Marvel superheroes team. With the world's weather out of control, the Avengers must break into teams and trek across the globe to shut down the network of Weather Towers that are now under the control of Red Skull. It's a high-stakes adventure with Captain America and War Machine in the desert - with the help of Black Panther and Thor - Iron Man and Justin Hammer in the jungle, and Black Widow, Hulk and Hawkeye in America's farmland.

programme.title: LEGO Marvel Avengers: Climate Conundrum
programme.desc: Animated series based on the Marvel superheroes team. Hulk, Black Widow and Hawkeye must protect New York City from a super-storm while Iron Man, Captain America, and Justin Hammer journey to a secret base hidden deep within the ocean to find Red Skull. Captain America is determined to take down Red Skull once and for all, but it will take the teamwork of rivals Iron Man and Justin Hammer to take down AIM, destroy Tony's Weather Machine, and save the world - assuming they can stop arguing...

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When a pub landlady is crushed to death by a giant box during a magic show organised by famous illusionist Gideon Latimer, pathologist Kate discovers the cables had been deliberately sabotaged. Evangelising curate Andrew Maplin claims the tragedy is God's vengeance for the pagan traditions still held deep in Midsomer Oaks, but Barnaby and Nelson fear Gideon was the real target. Neil Dudgeon, Gwilym Lee and Tamzin Malleson star in the murder mystery, with Joe Absolom, Andrew-Lee Potts, Justin Salinger, Amanda Burton and Stephanie Leonidas

programme.title: Ainsley's Food We Love
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott is joined by Christopher Biggins, Martine McCutcheon and Brian Turner for this Christmas special. On the menu are festive bubble patties, Thai turkey and coconut broth with chilli, coconut and coriander scones, and chocolate bread and butter pudding with spiced cherries. Plus, drinks expert Olly Smith is on hand to knock up some festive tipples, including an alcoholic chocolate milkshake

programme.title: The Masked Singer
programme.desc: Joel Dommett hosts the return of the singing contest, in which a celebrity panel and a studio audience must guess the famous faces performing behind elaborate costumes. Rita Ora, Jonathan Ross and Davina McCall all return on the panel, joined by newcomer Mo Gilligan, and this opening episode sees the first six mystery celebrities battling it out - Robin v Alien, Swan v Dragon and Sausage v Badger

programme.title: In for a Penny
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts the game show from locations around the UK, setting members of the public unusual challenges

programme.title: Despicable Me (2010)
programme.desc: A criminal mastermind hopes to seal his reputation as the greatest villain of them all by stealing the Moon. First he needs to get his hands on a shrinking ray owned by a rival evil genius, and adopts three orphan girls to help him - but finds that fatherhood brings out his long-buried caring side. Animated comedy, with the voices of Steve Carell, Jason Segel, Russell Brand and Julie Andrews

programme.title: Jurassic World (2015)
programme.desc: Decades on from the creation of Jurassic Park, a fully operational attraction filled with genetically engineered dinosaurs has been opened on the island. In need of a new attraction, the company behind the park creates an entirely new species - only for it to escape and terrorise the tourists. Action adventure sequel, starring Chris Pratt and Bryce Dallas Howard

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents an hour of laughs, mistakes and outtakes from the quiz that were never meant to see the light of day. With contributions from chasers Anne Hegerty, Darragh Ennis, Mark Labbett, Jenny Ryan, Paul Sinha and Shaun Wallace

programme.title: The Story of SM:TV Live
programme.desc: Ant and Dec reunite with Cat Deeley to look back on making the Saturday morning kids' show, revisiting the studio where the series was filmed and reminiscing about their favourite segments and running gags, including Wonky Donkey, Chums, and Dec's obsession with Sabrina the Teenage Witch. Featuring classic clips and contributions from guests and celebrity fans, including Kylie Minogue, Stephen Mulhern, Fleur East and Scarlett Moffatt

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: Host Jeremy Clarkson turns up the pressure for Prue Leith, Kym Marsh and Ronni Ancona as they try to answer 15 questions with the help of four lifelines to win a million pounds for their chosen charities

programme.title: The Silence of the Lambs (1991)
programme.desc: Trainee FBI agent Clarice Starling is sent to interview an imprisoned psychopath with a taste for eating his victims. The inmate offers to help catch a serial killer who is holding a politician's daughter hostage - but to learn what he knows, Clarice has to engage in his dangerous mind games. Jonathan Demme's Oscar-winning thriller, based on Thomas Harris's novel, starring Jodie Foster, Anthony Hopkins, Scott Glenn and Ted Levine

programme.title: Motorsport UK
programme.desc: John Neil presents action from Thruxton, including round 11 of the GT4 Supercup and round 13 of the Mini Challenge Trophy

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Robozuna
programme.desc: Children's sci-fi fantasy series about 14-year-old orphan Ariston and his robot Mangle, fighting an evil empire together

programme.title: The Powerpuff Girls
programme.desc: When Buttercup loses the Professor's latest invention, she'll have to beat The Gangreen Gang at their own game to get it back

programme.title: The Powerpuff Girls
programme.desc: An alarmingly familiar cat burglar terrorizes Townsville.

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: Iris isn't having the best day, so Mike decides to try and make her feel better by putting on a circus on the patio

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: Stephan brings a robotic dog home, and Mike decides to use it as a stunt double so he can impress Iris.

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: On a very hot day, Mike invites Iris to join him for a cooling cocktail on the terrace, but disaster strikes when the raccoons steal the ice maker.

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: Following a bump to the head, Mercury seems to be acting like a dog, much to Mike's surprise

programme.title: Buck & Buddy
programme.desc: Beetle Buck and stick insect Buddy are the best of friends who like to collect unusual and interesting things from the strange world in which they live.

programme.title: LEGO Hidden Side: Night of the Harbinger
programme.desc: Jack and Parker chase a fire ghost to discover a strange silvery substance, a clue that something is amiss in Newbury. Parker comes face to face with the terrifying Harbinger, who ominously tells her that 'this world is doomed', then Jack and Parker find the Harbinger as he summons the world-ending Maw, so the kids decide that it's time to end the Maw once and for all.

programme.title: The Rubbish World of Dave Spud
programme.desc: The misadventures of underdog Dave Spud and his family. Gran sits in front of the television for so long that she turns into a cocoon, and with a little help from Dave - who wants his favourite seat back - she hatches into a moth. When Moth-Gran sets her sights on the moon, it's up to the Spuds to find a way to entice her back.

programme.title: The Rubbish World of Dave Spud
programme.desc: Gran needs new teeth. Probably not wise to nab a set found on the side of the road though, under a full moon.

programme.title: Jack Frost (1998)
programme.desc: A rock musician who realises he is spending too much time away from his family is killed in a car accident while travelling home for the festive season. The following year his spirit takes on the form of a snowman, made by his son, allowing them to spend one last Christmas together. Fantasy drama, starring Michael Keaton, Kelly Preston, Joseph Cross and Mark Addy

programme.title: National Lampoon's Christmas Vacation (1989)
programme.desc: An American householder is dismayed when his plans for a traditional festive family holiday are thrown into complete disarray by a succession of annoying neighbours and long-lost relatives, who turn up on his doorstep offering their own brand of Christmas cheer. Comedy, starring Chevy Chase, Beverly D'Angelo, Randy Quaid, Diane Ladd, Juliette Lewis, Miriam Flynn, Johnny Galecki and William Hickey

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: It's the day of the Little Crosby Folk Festival and organiser Toby Winning is found dead, having drowned in a bowl of eggs and live eels. The murder seems to have been inspired by a ballad made famous by the late, lamented star Johnny Carver. Then it comes to light the victim recently announced plans to move the event to a new location, which would have cost local entrepreneur Frank Wainwright dearly. Toby's estranged wife Alice also comes under suspicion, before two further killings take place, echoing more lyrics from the song

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Five workmates answer questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then try to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money. Hosted by Warwick Davis

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Matt, John, Laura and Barry pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Charity and Mack's plan backfires, Priya meddles, and it remains to be seen whether Charity and Chas will be able to patch things up

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Daniel calls an ambulance when Peter is taken ill, Adam accuses Sarah of shopping Gary to the authorities, and Todd shows off his new man

programme.title: Bradley Walsh & Son: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: The actor and his son Barney reflect on their travels around America, picking their favourite moments and revealing previously unaired footage of their adventures. Armed with popcorn and refreshments, the Walshes watch back everything from additional unseen footage to moments they captured on their phones, behind-the- scenes clips with the crew and compilations of their road-trip antics. These include more sing-along bangers from their playlist, Brad's jokes, their favourite snacks, as well as quizzes and games they played to pass the time

programme.title: Billy Connolly: It's Been a Pleasure
programme.desc: Celebration of the comedian's greatest performances, made to mark his retirement from stand-up. Featuring interviews with Billy and his wife Pamela Stephenson at their home in the Florida Keys, and contributions from famous fans including Paul McCartney, Elton John, Lenny Henry, Dustin Hoffman, Russell Brand, Whoopi Goldberg, Aisling Bea, Armando Iannucci and Sheridan Smith

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Katherine Jenkins: Christmas Spectacular from the Royal Albert Hall
programme.desc: The mezzo-soprano presents a concert of seasonal favourites, captured live in London, with guests including bass-baritone Bryn Terfel, Italian tenor Alberto Urso, American Broadway star Marisha Wallace, English National Ballet lead principal Erina Takahashi, the Royal Air Force Regiment Band and actors Vanessa Redgrave and Bill Nighy

programme.title: All Elite Wrestling: Dynamite
programme.desc: Hard-hitting action from the world of All Elite Wrestling, featuring all of the biggest stars on the roster, including Adam Page, Chris Jericho, Cody and Jon Moxley

programme.title: Zone of Champions
programme.desc: Comedy clip show, in which a pair of sports commentators (played by John Thomson and Jessica Knappett) narrate home movie footage as if they were sporting events. The first episode features highlights from the World Series of Escalator Racing and the sheep-based Rugby Wool Cup

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Craig Charles chats about his New Year show on 6 Music, putting his live DJ sets on hold due to Covid-19, and battling seasickness on the choppy waves in Don't Rock The Boat

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip. Presented by Lorraine Kelly

programme.title: Celebrity Supermarket Sweep
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal hosts a star-studded edition of the game show, with teams consisting of Emmerdale stars Charley Webb and Matthew Wolfenden, The Chase's Jenny Ryan and singer Megan McKenna, and DJs Melvin Odoom and Rickie Haywood-Williams racing down the aisles to win a £10,000 jackpot for the charity of their choice

programme.title: Happy Feet (2006)
programme.desc: A young penguin disappoints his fellow seabirds with his inability to sing - but instead he displays a remarkable talent for tap dancing. However, his elders refuse to accept this outlandish behaviour and send the youngster into exile, marking the start of a wonderful journey. Animated adventure, with the voices of Elijah Wood, Robin Williams, Nicole Kidman and Hugh Jackman

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The launch of the latest sparkling wine by Midsomer Vinae Winery falls flat when critic Nadia Simons gives it a scathing review, then guests sampling the vintage start to collapse. Owner William Carnarvon suspects the Farmers' Wives Association of trying to destroy his business, while his wife Diana frets over her mother Matilda, one of the victims. Pathologist Kate reveals the drink was laced with slug poison, but how is the attack connected to the death of a child in a hit-and-run? Barnaby and Nelson are called in to investigate. Neil Dudgeon and Gwilym Lee star in the mystery drama, with Claire Bloom, Mark Bonnar, Rosie Cavaliero and Ruth Gemmell

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Warwick Davis in which a team of five workmates answers questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then tries to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. Josh, Steven, Amelia and Lynne work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Marlon feels lonely, Mack has a proposition, and it remains to be seen whether Al's secret will be exposed

programme.title: Wonder Woman (2017)
programme.desc: An Amazon princess has her first encounter with the outside world during the First World War, when an American airman crash lands on her isolated island home. Determined to bring the chaos sweeping the world to an end, she ventures onto the front line in the war to end all wars to bring down an ancient and powerful evil force that is driving the human race towards destruction. Superhero fantasy adventure, starring Gal Gadot, Chris Pine and David Thewlis

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: It's Clarkson on TV
programme.desc: The acerbic host passes judgement on what the great British public watched in 2020 - and didn't - and highlights some fascinating foreign TV shows that managed to make it to air. Shows under his gaze include gripping dramas White House Farm, Quiz and Des, as well as the latest offerings from the streaming services including dating shows such as Love Is Blind and property shows like Selling Sunset

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: Good Morning Britain anchor Piers Morgan and Diversity's Jordan Banjo take up the challenge, hoping to win as much as £1miilion for their chosen charities. As ever, lifelines including Phone a Friend, Ask the Host and 50-50 are available to them if they get stuck. Presented by Jeremy Clarkson

programme.title: Jennifer Saunders' Memory Lane
programme.desc: Jennifer slips behind the wheel of a classic red Jaguar E-Type, joined in the passenger seat by Hollywood actor Michael Sheen whom she quizzes about his life and times. Heading to Port Talbot where Michael grew up, they stop at key locations to prompt personal stories from his childhood right up to the present day. Drawing on evocative family photos and colourful local archive, Michael's recollections are by turns funny, surprising and incredibly poignant

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: When 39-year-old Durham dad Philip Hunter discovered he had terminal cancer, he got in touch with Alan Titchmarsh and his team to build a special garden for him, his wife Sam and two boys, Ethan and Josh to enjoy in his final years. However, when tragedy strikes earlier than expected, Alan and his colleagues do their best to create the garden Philip would have wanted. By using some clever design, the team fills the modestly sized plot with elegant features and details, but will the family like the result?

programme.desc: Joel Dommett hosts the return of the singing contest, in which a celebrity panel and a studio audience must guess the famous faces performing behind elaborate costumes. Rita Ora, Jonathan Ross and Davina McCall all return on the panel, joined by newcomer Mo Gilligan, and this opening episode sees the first six mystery celebrities battling it out - Robin v Alien, Swan v Dragon and Sausage v Badger

programme.title: In for a Penny
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts the game show from locations around the UK, setting members of the public unusual challenges

programme.title: Despicable Me (2010)
programme.desc: A criminal mastermind hopes to seal his reputation as the greatest villain of them all by stealing the Moon. First he needs to get his hands on a shrinking ray owned by a rival evil genius, and adopts three orphan girls to help him - but finds that fatherhood brings out his long-buried caring side. Animated comedy, with the voices of Steve Carell, Jason Segel, Russell Brand and Julie Andrews

programme.title: Jurassic World (2015)
programme.desc: Decades on from the creation of Jurassic Park, a fully operational attraction filled with genetically engineered dinosaurs has been opened on the island. In need of a new attraction, the company behind the park creates an entirely new species - only for it to escape and terrorise the tourists. Action adventure sequel, starring Chris Pratt and Bryce Dallas Howard

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents an hour of laughs, mistakes and outtakes from the quiz that were never meant to see the light of day. With contributions from chasers Anne Hegerty, Darragh Ennis, Mark Labbett, Jenny Ryan, Paul Sinha and Shaun Wallace

programme.title: The Story of SM:TV Live
programme.desc: Ant and Dec reunite with Cat Deeley to look back on making the Saturday morning kids' show, revisiting the studio where the series was filmed and reminiscing about their favourite segments and running gags, including Wonky Donkey, Chums, and Dec's obsession with Sabrina the Teenage Witch. Featuring classic clips and contributions from guests and celebrity fans, including Kylie Minogue, Stephen Mulhern, Fleur East and Scarlett Moffatt

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: Host Jeremy Clarkson turns up the pressure for Prue Leith, Kym Marsh and Ronni Ancona as they try to answer 15 questions with the help of four lifelines to win a million pounds for their chosen charities

programme.title: The Silence of the Lambs (1991)
programme.desc: Trainee FBI agent Clarice Starling is sent to interview an imprisoned psychopath with a taste for eating his victims. The inmate offers to help catch a serial killer who is holding a politician's daughter hostage - but to learn what he knows, Clarice has to engage in his dangerous mind games. Jonathan Demme's Oscar-winning thriller, based on Thomas Harris's novel, starring Jodie Foster, Anthony Hopkins, Scott Glenn and Ted Levine

programme.desc: John Neil presents action from Thruxton, including round 11 of the GT4 Supercup and round 13 of the Mini Challenge Trophy

programme.title: The Powerpuff Girls
programme.desc: An alarmingly familiar cat burglar terrorizes Townsville.

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: Iris isn't having the best day, so Mike decides to try and make her feel better by putting on a circus on the patio

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: Stephan brings a robotic dog home, and Mike decides to use it as a stunt double so he can impress Iris.

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: On a very hot day, Mike invites Iris to join him for a cooling cocktail on the terrace, but disaster strikes when the raccoons steal the ice maker.

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: Following a bump to the head, Mercury seems to be acting like a dog, much to Mike's surprise

programme.title: Buck & Buddy
programme.desc: Beetle Buck and stick insect Buddy are the best of friends who like to collect unusual and interesting things from the strange world in which they live.

programme.title: LEGO Hidden Side: Night of the Harbinger
programme.desc: Jack and Parker chase a fire ghost to discover a strange silvery substance, a clue that something is amiss in Newbury. Parker comes face to face with the terrifying Harbinger, who ominously tells her that 'this world is doomed', then Jack and Parker find the Harbinger as he summons the world-ending Maw, so the kids decide that it's time to end the Maw once and for all.

programme.title: The Rubbish World of Dave Spud
programme.desc: The misadventures of underdog Dave Spud and his family. Gran sits in front of the television for so long that she turns into a cocoon, and with a little help from Dave - who wants his favourite seat back - she hatches into a moth. When Moth-Gran sets her sights on the moon, it's up to the Spuds to find a way to entice her back.

programme.title: The Rubbish World of Dave Spud
programme.desc: Gran needs new teeth. Probably not wise to nab a set found on the side of the road though, under a full moon.

programme.title: Jack Frost (1998)
programme.desc: A rock musician who realises he is spending too much time away from his family is killed in a car accident while travelling home for the festive season. The following year his spirit takes on the form of a snowman, made by his son, allowing them to spend one last Christmas together. Fantasy drama, starring Michael Keaton, Kelly Preston, Joseph Cross and Mark Addy

programme.title: National Lampoon's Christmas Vacation (1989)
programme.desc: An American householder is dismayed when his plans for a traditional festive family holiday are thrown into complete disarray by a succession of annoying neighbours and long-lost relatives, who turn up on his doorstep offering their own brand of Christmas cheer. Comedy, starring Chevy Chase, Beverly D'Angelo, Randy Quaid, Diane Ladd, Juliette Lewis, Miriam Flynn, Johnny Galecki and William Hickey

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: It's the day of the Little Crosby Folk Festival and organiser Toby Winning is found dead, having drowned in a bowl of eggs and live eels. The murder seems to have been inspired by a ballad made famous by the late, lamented star Johnny Carver. Then it comes to light the victim recently announced plans to move the event to a new location, which would have cost local entrepreneur Frank Wainwright dearly. Toby's estranged wife Alice also comes under suspicion, before two further killings take place, echoing more lyrics from the song

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Five workmates answer questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then try to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money. Hosted by Warwick Davis

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase

These include more sing-along bangers from their playlist, Brad's jokes, their favourite snacks, as well as quizzes and games they played to pass the time

programme.desc: The launch of the latest sparkling wine by Midsomer Vinae Winery falls flat when critic Nadia Simons gives it a scathing review, then guests sampling the vintage start to collapse. Owner William Carnarvon suspects the Farmers' Wives Association of trying to destroy his business, while his wife Diana frets over her mother Matilda, one of the victims. Pathologist Kate reveals the drink was laced with slug poison, but how is the attack connected to the death of a child in a hit-and-run? Barnaby and Nelson are called in to investigate. Heading to Port Talbot where Michael grew up, they stop at key locations to prompt personal stories from his childhood right up to the present day. Drawing on evocative family photos and colourful local archive, Michael's recollections are by turns funny, surprising and incredibly poignant

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: When 39-year-old Durham dad Philip Hunter discovered he had terminal cancer, he got in touch with Alan Titchmarsh and his team to build a special garden for him, his wife Sam and two boys, Ethan and Josh to enjoy in his final years. However, when tragedy strikes earlier than expected, Alan and his colleagues do their best to create the garden Philip would have wanted. By using some clever design, the team fills the modestly sized plot with elegant features and details, but will the family like the result?

programme.title: Emmerdale
