programme.desc: Peralta and Santiago go undercover as an engaged couple in a bid to catch an identity thief, and find that the investigation brings them closer together. Meanwhile, Boyle organises a surprise birthday party for Rosa. Comedy, with Andy Samberg and Melissa Fumero

programme.title: Young Sheldon
programme.desc: When Sheldon is bullied by an unlikely character, Mary and George disagree on how to handle the situation, contemplating whether they should their son employ self-defence techniques. Undeterred, he tries his hand at jiu-jitsu and even covers himself in bubble-wrap for protection

programme.title: Young Sheldon
programme.desc: When Mary bans Sheldon from reading a comic book that she considers to be too mature for him, the youngster decides it's time to stop living under his mother's thumb

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard is caught between a rock and a hard place when he is made responsible for distributing extra grant money. Bernadette turns the back garden playhouse into a hideaway to escape from her husband and children

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon tries to befriend Amy's father, but when Howard lures Mr Fowler away with his magic tricks Sheldon has no choice but to bond with Mrs Fowler. Meanwhile, Raj and Anu attempt to get physical. Kathy Bates and Teller guest star

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: John Paul is fearful when he speaks to George about the dangerous situation unfolding in the village. Elsewhere, Ollie's actions threaten to tear Cindy and Luke apart

programme.title: Rude(ish) Tube
programme.desc: Comedy clips showcasing amusing internet videos on the themes of food and animals

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Former contestants Jane Beedle and Andrew Smyth from 2016, and Liam Charles and Flo Atkins from 2017 join Sandi Toksvig and Noel Fielding for festive-themed challenges. It's down to judges Paul Hollywood and Prue Leith to decide who wins the Christmas Star Baker title. Plus, British rockers The Darkness perform their hit, Christmas Time (Don't Let the Bells End)

programme.title: Jason Bourne (2016)
programme.desc: Former government operative Jason Bourne has been in hiding for 12 years after exposing a sinister CIA operation. However, he once again finds himself being hunted down when a former CIA operative gone rogue acquires top secret information - including details of Bourne's own father's involvement in a clandestine scheme. Action thriller, starring Matt Damon, Tommy Lee Jones, Alicia Vikander and Julia Stiles

programme.title: The Inbetweeners
programme.desc: Will plans to celebrate his birthday with a grown-up dinner, but his friends face a dilemma when they discover the event clashes with a more popular party. They end up gatecrashing the rival gathering, only to find that Simon's French exchange student steals the limelight

programme.title: The Inbetweeners
programme.desc: Will tries to reinvent the boys' image by taking them clubbing around London, but Simon is unsure whether to go until Carli shows an interest in the outing. When the friends arrive in the West End, their efforts to appear cool are hampered by aggressive bouncers, a tramp and some angry bus passengers

programme.title: Derry Girls
programme.desc: Erin, Clare, Michelle, Orla and James are beside themselves with excitement as they have tickets to see boyband Take That in Belfast. When Ma Mary forbids them to go, they decide that they will stop at nothing to get there and see their idols in concert. Comedy from writer Lisa McGee, set against the Troubles in early-1990s Northern Ireland, starring Saoirse-Monica Jackson, Nicola Coughlan, Jamie-Lee O'Donnell, Louisa Harland and Dylan Llewellyn

programme.title: Derry Girls
programme.desc: The Quinns cause uproar at a family wedding, and when the girls - and James - fail to behave themselves at the reception, the consequences are very serious indeed, especially for poor cousin Eammon (played by guest star Ardal O'Hanlon). Comedy from writer Lisa McGee, set against the Troubles in early-1990s Northern Ireland, starring Saoirse-Monica Jackson, Nicola Coughlan, Jamie-Lee O'Donnell, Louisa Harland and Dylan Llewellyn

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Raj is racked with guilt when he tries to date Lucy and Emily at the same time, Penny has trouble deciding whether to take a role in a film, and Sheldon struggles to choose between two gaming systems. Comedy, starring Kunal Nayyar, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Penny tries to help Sheldon when he starts to wonder whether he is wasting his time studying string theory. Meanwhile, during a dinner, Raj discovers Howard once embarrassed himself on a date with Emily. Stephen Hawking guest stars

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox
programme.desc: Loyle Carner and his mother Jean, Shaun Ryder and Bez, Chris Kamara and his sons Jack and Ben, Example and Professor Green, Olly Alexander and his mother Vicki, Rylan Clark-Neal and his mother Linda, Denise Van Outen and her boyfriend Eddie Boxshall, Oti Mabuse and her sisters Motsi and Phemelo, Chris Eubank Sr and Chris Eubank Jr, Martin and Roman Kemp, Laurence and Emilia Fox, Gyles Brandreth and Sheila Hancock, Emily Atack and her mother Kate Robbins, and Nick Grimshaw and his niece Liv critique TV airing in mid-July 2019

programme.title: Angel
programme.desc: Lorne's spell to restore Cordelia's memory causes the gang members to revert to their teenage selves, leaving them with no memories of each other. Supernatural drama, starring David Boreanaz

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard's purchase of a dining-room table causes Sheldon to re-evaluate his life. Meanwhile, Howard is offered the chance to go back to the International Space Station, and Bernadette struggles to decide whether to encourage him. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Melissa Rauch

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: After arguing with Sheldon, Howard tries to make amends by taking him to Nasa's headquarters in Houston. Elsewhere, Penny has second thoughts about quitting her job as a waitress when her car dies, while Amy tries to find Raj a date. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco

programme.title: Melissa & Joey
programme.desc: Elena faces being deported, so Joe offers to marry her so she can stay in the country. Mel refuses to take part in the scheme, but when things begin to go wrong she is forced to protect the couple. Comedy, starring Melissa Joan Hart and Joey Lawrence

programme.title: Sabrina the Teenage Witch
programme.desc: Sabrina casts a spell on Josh and Harvey to stop them fighting for her affection - but it works so well that they become best friends and begin to ignore her altogether. After four years of enduring Sabrina's spells, Harvey finally suspects she's a witch

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance for viewers to purchase goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Kirstie's Vintage Gems
programme.desc: In this bite-sized episode, Kirstie Allsopp shows viewers some quirky ways to dress a dining table, and reveals how to make a paper napkin rose and a cupcake bouquet. The presenter also demonstrates a recipe for chocolate bowls

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty visits the largest wholesale food market in the world in Paris and discovers why British cheese sales are soaring across Europe. Matt Tebbutt travels to one of the largest ham producers in Italy and meets a pig farmer who tells him about a delicate problem that all pork producers face, while Kate Quilton investigates why some limes are so much juicier than others

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: The architect attends a carol service in a 130-year-old flat-pack tin church, while Will Hardie visits a very different chapel that has been transformed into a luxury home. The duo help an ice carver create a winter wonderland-themed bar in Liverpool, and the programme catches up with some of George's favourite builds of the year. There are also a few Christmas drinks in the garden of Joel Bird, winner of the Shed of the Year prize

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: Architect George Clarke and master craftsman Will Hardie find out how Norway, a country with some of the harshest weather on the planet, has become a leader in architectural design. Buildings include a treehouse that can withstand nine tons of snowfall and gale force winds, and an ice hotel complete with 30 rooms, a bar and even a snow chapel. Plus, a moveable home that has kept members of its community safe for hundreds of years

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: Architect George Clarke and master craftsman Will Hardie embark on a road trip to explore the ingenious small-space wonders hidden away among the snowy peaks of the Swiss, French and Italian Alps. They start out from an observation point 4,000 metres above sea level, then take in a secret ski lodge disguised as a boulder, a treehouse resembling a giant acorn and a Swiss cabin made almost entirely from concrete. Other highlights include a hamlet made from eight 200-year-old chalets collected from all over the Alps and a mountaintop restaurant resembling a spaceship

programme.title: Gentlemen Prefer Blondes (1953)
programme.desc: Lounge singers Lorelei and Dorothy embark on a transatlantic cruise to Paris in search of rich husbands. While Dorothy falls for a penniless man, Lorelei thinks she has found the millionaire of her dreams, only for his father to hire a private eye to spy on her and prove she is a gold-digger. Howard Hawks' musical comedy, starring Marilyn Monroe, Jane Russell, Charles Coburn and Elliott Reid

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition starts at the Inn at the Park in Aberdeen, where owners Ronnie and Steph Caird hope to impress their guests and be named best value for money at the end of the week

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit of the week is to Renfrewshire and Ingliston Country Club, where events manager Shaunna Kirk and sales manager Fiona Gallagher are a force to be reckoned with

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: For the third visit, the group heads to 1692 Wasdale in the Lake District, where owners Steve and Allard Newell have proudly built their B&B from the ground up

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The guests stay at the Lynbar Hotel in Blackpool, where Ronnie and Steph have parking problems, while confident owner John O'Donnell boasts of serving the best breakfast in town

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time to settle some scores. Inn at the Park owners Ronnie and Steph Caird are intent on clearing up John's issues with the toilet

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Personal trainer Eve Booth hosts the first dinner party in Co Durham and Wearside, expressing concern that her starter of haggis and black pudding may not be to everyone's taste. Although her main course gets a lukewarm reception, the biggest threat to the evening seems to be fellow contestant Ken Brown's taste in jokes

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Interior designer and gypsy wedding supplier Paula Thompson hosts the second party in Co Durham and Wearside, and hopes to bring a touch of class to the proceedings with her 'black and bling' themed event. The evening's menu includes fresh lobster and hairy prawns, accompanied by a spot of belly dancing

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Nineteen-year-old languages student Donal Anand-Shaw hosts the third dinner party in Co Durham and Wearside, and decides to make the event black tie. He also arranges for a string quartet to play, and serves up a locally-sourced scallop starter

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Debt collector Eleri Watson hosts the fourth dinner party in Co Durham and Wearside. However, her efforts to organise an all-Welsh evening do not go according to plan when soil is found in the leek soup and lamb is served burned, dampening the guests' enthusiasm

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Businessman Ken Brown hosts the final dinner party in Co Durham and Wearside, and hopes to entertain his guests with a 'wear a wig' themed evening. After annoying his fellow contestants over the past week, he hopes to make amends with his unique entertainment, before the winner of the £1,000 prize is announced

programme.title: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: The second of two documentaries reliving the devastation caused by the weather throughout the winter of 2013 to 2014. This programme looks at the flooding that caused misery for thousands of homeowners across the nation, with eyewitness testimony and expert commentary, and asks if the nation should prepare for more of the same in years to come

programme.title: 999: What's Your Emergency?
programme.desc: Police and paramedics in Cheshire respond to more incidents, including a 20-year-old who has returned home after a night out, collapsed in front of her parents and stopped breathing. Plus, a man calls the emergency services to say that a naked stranger his mother met at a punk gig has fallen down their stairs

programme.title: 999: What's Your Emergency?
programme.desc: Across the UK, around 2,200 women go into labour every day. Most make it to hospital, but for those who cannot, the ambulance service is there to help. The programme features call handlers giving worried partners advice while staff race to deliver the baby, and reveals how some new parents overreact and call 999 at the first sign of a cough or a bump to the head

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Patients whose visions of the world are rocked by life-changing events, including 44-year-old Sergio, who is flown in by air ambulance after a cycling accident. Another cyclist - 71-year-old Alan - arrives in resus after being in a collision with a van. Doctors suspect a head injury and multiple fractures. Alan's wife Jenny reveals how she met him and how a sudden accident involving her son burst their idyll and took their lives on an unexpected path

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: This unique fusion of two popular Channel 4 shows receives a New Year outing as Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy panel show's version of the words-and-numbers quiz, with a Hogmanay flavour. Jon Richardson and Richard Osman take on Johnny Vegas and Sara Pascoe. Meanwhile, David O'Doherty joins Countdown's resident lexicographer Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner to arbitrate over the players' collective efforts and Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters with Joe Wilkinson

programme.title: 999: What's Your Emergency?
programme.desc: Police and paramedics in Cheshire respond to more incidents, including a 20-year-old who has returned home after a night out, collapsed in front of her parents and stopped breathing. Plus, a man calls the emergency services to say that a naked stranger his mother met at a punk gig has fallen down their stairs

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Meat-loving Jimmy Doherty attempts to go vegan for a week in the USA. While he's there, he sees if there's a solution to carbon emissions from belching cows. Plus, Matt Tebbutt sets out to discover why some vegan products are more expensive than their meaty equivalents, Dr Helen Lawal asks if we should raise children as vegan, and Kate Quilton delves into the secret world of the vegan cheese melt

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: The Craggy Island clerics face the prospect of an untimely death during the return flight from a visit to a holy shrine. Jack quickly commandeers the only parachute and bails out, taking the drinks trolley with him - leaving Ted and Dougal to their fate

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance for viewers to purchase goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance for viewers to purchase goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Kirstie's Handmade Treasures
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp meets the ladies at the Merched y Wawr - the Welsh version of the Women's Institute - and they show her how to make petals using gelatine

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Although she's approaching retirement age, Karmen Meredith is in no mood to slow down. Instead, the Derbyshire woman is looking for a bolthole on Spain's Costa del Sol, so Danni Menzies shows her five properties to choose between

programme.title: Cocoon (1985)
programme.desc: The elderly residents of a retirement home treat themselves to an occasional dip in the pool at an abandoned house. It turns out that friendly aliens in human form are using the place to restore dormant members of their species - and energy from their cocoons is giving the old-timers a new lease of life. Ron Howard's sci-fi drama, with Wilford Brimley, Jessica Tandy, Hume Cronyn, Don Ameche and Steve Guttenberg

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Dog-mad executive PA Julie-Ann Rayner hosts the first dinner party in Southend-on-Sea, Essex, and hopes to impress her guests - science teacher Damian Loneragan, DJ Scott Langley, barmaid Steph Barrett and glamorous granny Carol Dolman - with a 'taste of the sea' menu

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Carol Dolman hosts the second dinner party in Southend-on-Sea, Essex. The glamorous grandmother hopes her scallops and roast lamb, 'dress to impress' theme and tango lessons given by local instructors are enough to win her the £1,000 prize

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Teacher Damian Loneragan hosts the third dinner party in Southend-on-Sea, Essex, where he hopes his chilli-based dishes, and a lemon and ginger dessert, will help him win the £1,000 prize. However, after a disastrous main course, he goes all-out to wow his guests with science experiments in the garden

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Barmaid Steph Barrett is the fourth host of this week's competition in Southend-on-Sea, Essex, and sets a 1940s dress code. The day gets off to a tricky start when she attempts to make home-made pastry for the first time, but her nan saves the day. The guests are suitably impressed by her home, and the dancing entertainment goes down even better than the food

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: DJ Scott Langley hosts the final party in Southend-on-Sea, Essex, and decides to take a gamble by serving fried pigeon as his main course. He insists on an 'outrageous' dress code for the evening, and unveils some special entertainment before the week's winner is announced

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contest begins at the Shelley Guest House in Aberystwyth, where its husband-and-wife owners hope to prove that their establishment offers the best value for money by providing their guests with an old-school form of entertainment, some Spanish cuisine and a monumental continental breakfast

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The guests travel to the five-star Ixworth House in Bury St Edmunds, Suffolk, where hosts John and Sharyn Snell are quietly confident their establishment will impress. John reveals his hidden talents at dinner, but his performance at breakfast the next morning does not go down well with all of the fellow B&B owners

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: In York, part-time panto dame Graham Smith runs the Georgian House & Mews, and takes his guests back in time with a Nordic test of strength. At supper, Hazel has romance on her mind, but the guests are left to fend for themselves at breakfast - until Graham makes a show-stopping appearance

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to the George Hotel in Orton, Cumbria, for the fourth visit of the day. Owner Hazel McAleese organises a fishing trip as the afternoon activity, during which one of the guests decides to use the opportunity to give everyone a lesson in innuendo

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants featured throughout the contest reunite to revisit the highs and lows of their various stays, and as the B&B owners confront their critics face to face, they discover what the rival parties really thought of the quality of service they encountered. After the deliberations are over, the winning guest house is announced

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Dick and Angel as they host a very special wedding for close friends. Motorcycle-mad Essex couple Martin and Kim start their search to buy the perfect chateau but can't see eye to eye. Cash-strapped Tim and Rebecca call in their mates to help with restoration work. Fiona puts her B&B plans on hold as she races against time to prepare for her first wedding. With the reception set to be outside she needs to fix 40 windows on the ageing chateau facade

programme.title: Great British Car Journeys
programme.desc: Christopher Timothy and Peter Davison revisit the golden age of motoring. The first episode is a homecoming for Peter and Chris, as they return to the Yorkshire countryside and the villages where they filmed All Creatures Great and Small. Travelling their own Herriot trail from Scarborough to the Yorkshire Dales, they uncover the county's rich and often surprising motoring history, catch up with an old friend or two, and take a very special and nostalgic drive along the roads where their friendship began more than 40 years ago

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud meets Clinton Dall from West Sussex, a self-made businessman and father of four aiming to build one of the largest homes ever featured on the show. Dall wants the end project to be perfect - from the finish of his mirrored cruciform steel columns, to how the floor tiles line up with the dining room table, and is willing to spend whatever it takes to achieve his vision. However, the construction represents a huge risk, as down one road lies abject failure and financial ruin, while down the other, is the creation of something sublime and almost heroic

programme.title: Blitz: London's Firestorm
programme.desc: Documentary using dramatic reconstructions and CGI to depict the most catastrophic night of bombing in London during the Second World War. Tens of thousands of incendiary devices fell on the city during the attack on December 29, 1940 - an assault Hitler hoped would break the British people's resolve. Already accustomed to raids, most fled for shelters when the bombardment started - but emerged the next morning to find many had died in the attack

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Maureen, who's 66, is rushed to St George's struggling to breathe through her tracheostomy. Nurse Christine battles to clear her airway, while Maureen's husband Preston waits in the relatives' room. As doctors carry out further investigations, Preston joins Maureen and it's revealed how their world was turned upside down when Maureen was diagnosed with the neurological condition multiple system atrophy. Ten-year-old Ryan is brought into resus having fallen at school and suffered internal bleeding

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef travels to La Grange, a suburb of Chicago, to help the owners of Cafe 36, an upmarket bistro threatening to destroy the marriage of its owners due to the scale of their financial losses. The head chef's attitude to quality control is driving customers away, and it is up to Gordon Ramsay to turn the business around

programme.title: Paul Hollywood Eats Japan
programme.desc: The Great British Bake Off judge takes a culinary tour of Japan, discovering the importance of food in Japanese culture. He starts in Tokyo, where he gets a lesson in dining etiquette, visits themed restaurants, and discovers the growing fashion for dining out and even performing karaoke alone. He meets an Australian wrestler and heavy metal singer named Lady Beard, who joins him on a madcap karting ride around the city's streets

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Maureen, who's 66, is rushed to St George's struggling to breathe through her tracheostomy. Nurse Christine battles to clear her airway, while Maureen's husband Preston waits in the relatives' room. As doctors carry out further investigations, Preston joins Maureen and it's revealed how their world was turned upside down when Maureen was diagnosed with the neurological condition multiple system atrophy. Ten-year-old Ryan is brought into resus having fallen at school and suffered internal bleeding

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty looks into skyr, a mysterious yoghurt-like product that claims to be entirely fat-free, but has a surprisingly creamy texture, and Kate Quilton heads to one of the busiest oyster hatcheries in France, where she witnesses the amazing science that goes into providing oysters year-round. Matt Tebbutt wants to know if the dried strawberries in his cereal are real or just a clever imitation

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance for viewers to purchase goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance for viewers to purchase goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Kirstie's Handmade Treasures
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp visits Manhattan, where she creates candy canes and lollipops at artisan sugar crafter Papabubble, before heading back to the UK to learn how to make passion fruit marshmallows covered in chocolate and coconut

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Patrick and Corrine Duly from East Sussex first visited Lanzarote four years ago. They immediately fell in love with the island, and now that they've retired they're looking for a holiday home where they can escape to and spend more active winters in the sun. With a budget of £140,000, Scarlette Douglas shows them five properties in the south of the island

programme.title: The Return of the Pink Panther (1975)
programme.desc: The inept Inspector Clouseau sets out to unmask a notorious international jewel thief believed to be behind the theft of a priceless diamond - but anarchy, as ever, follows wherever he goes. Blake Edwards' comedy sequel, starring Peter Sellers, Christopher Plummer, Catherine Schell, Herbert Lom, Burt Kwouk and Graham Stark

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: University lecturer Amanda Sherratt is keen to impress as she hosts the first dinner party in Lincolnshire, joined by beetroot farmer Chris Moore, theatre student Jack Stone, retail manager Ali Roberts and fitness enthusiast Alex Coxon. Amanda takes a risk with her Japanese-themed evening, which includes a raw fish starter and sumo-wrestling for entertainment

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Retail manager Ali Roberts hosts the second dinner party in Lincolnshire, confident that her gourmet cuisine and elegant style will win her the £1,000 prize. However, one diner's conversation threatens to cast a shadow over the evening

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Farmer Chris Moore hosts the third dinner party in Lincolnshire, where he plans to wow his guests with a beetroot-themed dinner. However, it is not just his obsession with the vegetable that surprises his diners, as they soon discover he has some hidden talents

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On day four in Lincolnshire, Jack Stone plans to win over his guests with a creative evening during which they will have the chance to make their own food, but the DIY pizzas don't go to plan. Meanwhile, an interesting outfit from Alex Coxon gets the attention of Chris Moore

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: A week of competitive dining in Lincolnshire concludes as proud Geordie Alex Coxon invites fellow contestants into her home, where she invites guests to sample dishes with names favouring her distinctive dialect. However, chaos in the kitchen hinders her chance of securing victory, and as the night draws to a close, the winner is announced

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contest begins at the Angel Inn in Wangford, Suffolk, where husband-and-wife owners Peter and Christine hope to prove that their establishment offers the best value for money. However, a self-service breakfast fails to impress the guests, and Christine struggles to keep her cool as confusion reigns in the kitchen

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second night of the competition moves to Moaps Farm in Dane Hill, East Sussex, where Lesley Miller hopes her guests will be charmed by her laid-back attitude. On arrival, the group is wowed by the view, but at breakfast the host's relaxed approach leads to disaster and some of her fellow B&B owners are forced to help out with the service

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: From Jabajak in Carmarthenshire, Wales, where sisters Lorraine Lloyd and Amanda Stuart-Robson host in their vineyard and restaurant B&B. The guests are stunned by the surroundings, and businessman Les Woollends and his head chef Lesz Zajac are convinced the sister act have the wow factor

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Les Woollends and his head chef Lesz Zajak welcome the contestants to the Clarendon Royal Hotel in Gravesend, Kent. The guests are dazzled on arrival, but Lesley Miller believes the champagne, truffles and fruit bowl are a ploy to win her over. The quality of the food gets good reviews at breakfast, although the guests poke fun at the crockery, and at feedback time criticism comes from an unexpected source and leaves the owner reconsidering his views of his rivals

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants featured throughout the week reunite to revisit the highs and lows of their various stays, and as the B&B owners confront their critics face to face, they discover what the rival parties really thought of the quality of service they encountered. After the deliberations are over, the winning guest house is announced

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Angel goes shopping with Stephanie and shows her the best way to frame her father's original artwork using modern and antique fabric. Justin and Annabelle put up their newly renovated exterior chateau shutters, but the numbering seems to have gone a bit haywire, so getting the shutters on the right windows proves a challenge

programme.title: Great British Car Journeys
programme.desc: Veteran actors Christopher Timothy and Peter Davison continue their adventures. The pair travel from the southern tip of the New Forest and along the Jurassic Coast, concluding at Burgh Island and visiting popular holiday destinations of the 1930s along the way. They also go camping 1930s style, recreate a historic hill climb and get behind the wheel of some classic cars, including a 1940s Willys Jeep, a Morris Cowley - Agatha Christie's favourite automobile - and 'Bessie', a car made famous by Doctor Who

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud returns to Devon for an update on an inspirational build. Royal Marine captain Jon White and his wife Becky were living happily in a small cottage when in June 2010, while serving in Afghanistan, Jon stepped on an improvised explosive device, losing three limbs. Every part of their lives had to change - starting with where they could live. Their architect came up with a cutting-edge, semi open-plan building with a dramatic wing-like-roof - and crucially no obvious signs of adaptations for Jon's disabilities. Has the new home proved as enabling for the couple and their expanding family as they had hoped?

programme.title: Chatsworth House: A Great British Year
programme.desc: Behind the scenes at the stately home belonging to the Duke and Duchess of Devonshire, following preparations for this year's Christmas festivities, planning for which begins at the height of summer. After months with no paying visitors due to Covid-19, the budget has had to be drastically cut, but the staff delve into the storerooms to recycle decorations from years past. Plans are also underway to make an ambitious light trail through the gardens

programme.title: A Very Royal Christmas: Sandringham Secrets
programme.desc: A look at how the royal family celebrate Christmas Day at the Queen's country estate in Norfolk, combining archive footage with interviews from royal insiders and former staff. They discuss the garish gifts, classy celebrations and historical traditions, as well as why Monopoly has been banned from royal Christmases for more than a decade

programme.title: Risen (2016)
programme.desc: A Roman officer and his assistant are charged with investigating what became of Jesus' body following the crucifixion, as the Roman hierarchy fears stories of a resurrected Messiah may lead to an uprising in Jerusalem. However, what they discover during their search forces them to question everything they believe. Biblical drama, starring Joseph Fiennes and Tom Felton

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy panel show's version of the words-and-numbers quiz, with Johnny Vegas and Rhod Gilbert taking on Joe Wilkinson and Roisin Conaty. Tom Allen joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner, while Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters

programme.title: Paul Hollywood Eats Japan
programme.desc: The baker visits one of the world's smallest restaurants in Hiroshima, where he gets a memorable cookery lesson from a survivor of the atomic bomb, and attends a rice festival in the snowy north of the country where local people fight with six-metre-long bamboo sticks to determine if they will get a good harvest. He also samples the buffet on a bullet train, discovers why Okinawa is embracing American fast food, and visits the ancient capital of Kyoto, where he attempts to develop a taste for tofu

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty visits Italy to assess why Parmesan costs up to twice as much as supermarket cheddar and Kate Quilton travels to Hungary and Spain to find out exactly where paprika comes from. Plus, Matt Tebbutt looks at the best and quickest ways to remove the shell from a hard-boiled egg

programme.title: Expedition with Steve Backshall

programme.title: Red Bull Soapbox Race
programme.desc: Teams battle against the clock in an extreme downhill race at the beautiful landscaped gardens of Villa Borghese, Rome

programme.title: World's Most Dangerous Roads
programme.desc: Hugh Dennis and David Baddiel travel along more than 1,200 miles of hazardous roads in Ethiopia on a journey to Aksum, the country's holiest place. However, they have to contend with more than fatigue and unreliable terrain to reach their destination, as they encounter drug-taking truck drivers, suffer a disastrous breakdown and start to run out of fuel at the worst possible time

programme.title: Top Gear: From A-Z
programme.desc: John Bishop narrates an alphabetical romp through the series' most memorable stunts and features as former hosts Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May pushed vehicles of all shapes and sizes to their limits. With 22 seasons of the show, there was no shortage of material to choose from as the likes of Tom Cruise and Cameron Diaz attempted to be the fastest Star in a Reasonably Priced Car, and the presenters risked life and limb in their assorted globe-trotting adventures

programme.title: Top Gear: Winter Blunderland

programme.title: Top Gear

programme.title: The Misadventures of Romesh Ranganathan
programme.desc: The comedian is in the Canadian Arctic for this festive special of the travelogue in which he explores countries rarely visited by tourists. He wants to know whether there is more to this area than the prospect of freezing to death or being eaten by a polar bear. Travelling across frozen wastelands with host Johnny Issaluk, he spends a week in the largest territory, getting to grips with the Inuit hunter's way of life - not an easy task for vegan Romesh. He also witnesses the erosion and resurgence of Inuit traditions and discover why alcohol is often restricted in the communities

programme.title: World's Most Dangerous Roads
programme.desc: Celebrities undertake some of the most perilous journeys in the world. Driving up Madagascar's eastern coast at the tail end of the rainy season is enough to test the best of friendships, but comedy actor Angus Deayton and broadcaster Mariella Frostrup are game enough to take on the challenge, and good enough friends to poke fun at each other along the way. The route has numerous river crossings and the duo face a variety of broken bridges and flimsy ferries as the only way across, while the roads themselves are mired with thick mud. Progress becomes increasingly painful and they are finally forced to spend a night in the car

programme.title: Travel Man: 48 Hours In
programme.desc: Richard Ayoade takes actress and writer Jessica Hynes on a whirlwind weekend away to Iceland, a country famous for spectacular water features, geo-thermic pools and a curious belief in elves - all attributes that the pair investigate with ruthless efficiency. They go on the popular Golden Circle Tour of the Nordic island's most famous waterfalls, glaciers and geysers - by helicopter, saving seven valuable hours. They also eat rotten shark and Brenavin, a spirit made from mashed potato, and attend elf school, followed by a speedy boat trip in search of whales. There's also a rapid chill-out at the Blue Lagoon geothermic pool and spa

programme.title: Travel Man: 48 Hours In
programme.desc: Richard Ayoade is joined by comedy actor Stephen Mangan for a weekend in the Moroccan city. Wanting to experience its famous street food, the pair sample a local delicacy of steamed sheep's head - an eye-opening culinary experience for them both. They then take a hot-air balloon flight over the Atlas Mountains with breathtaking views, watch a traditional story-telling performance and visit the historic Saadian tombs, as well as Marrakech's ancient tanneries. Finally, in search of a suitable place to sum up the holiday experience, Richard and Stephen head into the desert for a contemplative camel ride

Driving from the border with Azerbaijan to the Black Sea, Jessica soon discovers her spirit of adventure is causing Hugh a few moments of high anxiety, but there is worse to come when they find themselves stuck on a high mountain pass in bad weather and he has to overcome his vertigo. In order to do that, however, they have to drive across Burma, a country that has been largely closed to Westerners for more than 40 years, and must make the trip in three lorries, each bought sight unseen from the internet on a limited budget. What follows is an epic journey of beautiful scenery, regular adversity, ongoing malfunction and the constant bickering of the three men

programme.title: Travel Man: 48 Hours In
programme.desc: Richard Ayoade goes hurtling through a ruthlessly efficient mini-break in Paris with presenter and comedian Mel Giedroyc. Their base is a chic hotel famed for being the place where Oscar Wilde died, but there's no time to hang about soaking up the literary atmosphere, as they head to the Montparnasse Tower for arguably the best view of Paris, and one which has the bonus of including the Eiffel Tower. There's also calf's head casserole and snails, a scooter tour, 'green fairy' absinthe, a Cordon Bleu cookery school, bespoke perfume and an art tour

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: How the morale of the Third Reich, boosted by Germany's initial military successes, was shattered by the devastating defeat at Stalingrad, relentless Allied bombing raids and an attempt on Hitler's life. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Laurence Olivier narrates the story of the D-Day landings in June 1944, when the Allies launched the biggest ever amphibious invasion on the beaches of Normandy. Although both sides suffered heavy casualties, it proved to be the turning point in the war against Germany

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: The Nazi occupation of Holland, which began relatively peacefully with Dutch civil servants continuing to run their own country, but eventually resulted in many thousands of deaths through the attempted elimination of Jewish citizens and starvation of the non-Jewish inhabitants. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Laurence Olivier narrates an account of the liberation of Paris and Brussels in 1944, events which to many signified the end of the war. However, as the German forces retreated, Poland fell victim to one of the most tragic events of the war, Documentary chronicling the Second World War

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: The story of the Nazi quest to establish an Aryan race, which set out to subjugate the Jewish population with decrees and laws about citizenship and escalated to the use of execution camps and the horrors of the Holocaust. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.desc: Chronicling the events that brought about the demise of the Third Reich, as the Allies arrived at the gates of Berlin and Hitler boarded himself up in his bunker, where it is believed he committed suicide. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: Meat Loaf: In and Out of Hell
programme.desc: A profile of the rock star's life and career, reflecting on the music that propelled him to fame and charting his journey from an overweight, bullied child to his larger-than-life persona and icon status. Featuring an interview and performance by the singer, as well as backstage footage from a Las Vegas concert. The film also revisits the Dallas of Meat Loaf's early years and includes insights from high school friends who reveal where the unusual moniker really came from

programme.title: The Rolling Stones at the BBC
programme.desc: A selection of the band's performances from the BBC archives, in a programme originally broadcast in 2012 to celebrate their 50th anniversary. Songs include The Last Time, Let's Spend The Night Together, Get Off of My Cloud, Honky Tonk Women, Gimme Shelter, Miss You and Start Me Up. Plus, footage of the band performing 19th Nervous Breakdown on Top of the Pops in 1966, which was used in a 1960s BBC documentary about women with depression

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: The Enola Gay drops the first atomic bomb on Hiroshima in August 1945 as America bids to secure an unconditional surrender from the Japanese, even though conventional bombing raids had already flattened the country. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: Frozen Planet
programme.desc: How animals in the polar regions adapt to the summer weather. In a colony of 400,000 king penguins the adults go surfing and the chicks take mud-baths to cool off, while polar bear cubs take their first swimming lessons as the ice they inhabit melts away. A minke whale is hunted by a family of killer whales, succumbing to their battering after two hours, and Adelie penguins try to prevent their chicks being snatched by skuas

programme.title: Frozen Planet
programme.desc: With the arrival of autumn the Arctic Ocean begins freezing over, and polar bears gather in large numbers on the coast as they await the return of the ice. Two thousand beluga whales head for a particular estuary to thrash against the gravel and exfoliate, while in Antarctica, Adelie penguins take to the sea for the first time and face the danger of leopard seal attacks

programme.title: Frozen Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough examines how the arrival of winter sparks a ruthless battle for survival in the polar regions, with temperatures plummeting to -70C, winds reaching 120mph and day-long darkness. As a blizzard rages, a polar bear treks into the Arctic mountains to give birth, while the entire world population of spectacled eider braves the weather in a giant ice hole. Wolves and bison engage in battle, but alliances are also made, and beneath the snow, voles try to dodge the attacks of great grey owls and weasels

programme.title: Frozen Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough explores the life and work of people in the polar regions, including the residents of Norilsk in Siberia, the coldest city on Earth. He also discovers how the Danish armed forces maintain their country's claim to Greenland's natural resources by mounting a 2,000-mile dog sled patrol during the winter, and finds out how a research base at the South Pole is able to cope with being cut off from the outside world for six months at a time

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: A town in West Virginia that fell victim to the pace of change when steam trains became obsolete and an underground complex in Italy that had been abandoned for nearly 2,000 years

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo's journey from Buxton to London continues in Walsall, where he visits a leather business that still supplies the Queen with saddlery. He then explores how the centre of Birmingham has changed since Bradshaw's times, and is taught how to cook a curry by the owner of a leading Indian restaurant. Finally, he heads to the village of Bournville, where he learns how the railway transformed the fortunes of the Cadbury brothers

programme.title: The Blue Planet

programme.title: The Blue Planet

programme.title: The Blue Planet

As ever, the chefs must work out the secret techniques and recipes behind the household favourites before creating their own versions to be judged by a panel of Quality Street insiders and experts. But with so many to choose from, will they be expected to remake the green triangle, the orange crunch or the iconic purple one?

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Dick, Angel, Dorothy and Arthur, as well as Angel's parents Jenni and Steve prepare to celebrate a family Christmas in France. With an old 19th-century chateau ledger found in the attic and classic cookbooks to hand, the Strawbridges take inspiration from Christmases past, discovering what food, festivities, gifts and games would have been like in bygone eras. Dick and Angel's imagination goes into overdrive as they create a winter wonderland with an ice rink at its heart. Later, as the family settles down to dinner, so the sound of traditional hand bells rings out across the chateau and they wonder if their exploration of the past will create chateau traditions for the future

Howard Blake's score includes the award-winning song Walking in the Air, which was taken to number five in the charts by a young Aled Jones, but is here sung by Peter Auty

programme.title: Junior Bake Off
programme.desc: It's Pastry Day for the Heat B bakers and their last chance to win a place in Finals week. Judge Liam's Charles' technical challenge is an unusual hybrid requiring the bakers to demonstrate knowledge and techniques worthy of an advanced pastry chef. Then the bakers are tasked with creating a showstopper in the form of savoury hand pies. Hosted by Harry Hill

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps house hunters find their dream homes in popular sunny locations. Potential buyers explore the picturesque South Orlando region of Florida

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Tina Watkins has long struggled to get on the housing ladder in the UK. Now, she has decided to make the most of the incredibly good value property in Spain's Granada province and buy her first-ever home. With her best friend Jamie along to help, Jasmine Harman shows Tina just how far her £19,000 budget will stretch

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay travels to Fremont, California, in an attempt to save a restaurant that is tearing the owners' marriage apart. Saki Kavouniaris took over Spin a Yarn in 1995 and began to turn it into a success, before marrying bartender Jennifer. When the business started to fail, the couple decided to splash out on refurbishments to recover their glory days, but their efforts have been futile and now their relationship is at stake

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Chef Gordon Ramsay heads to Charlie's Italian bistro in La Verne, southern California, where the owner's lack of business nous is putting her relatives' homes at risk, after her mother and sister put them up as collateral. To make matters worse, the restaurant's food is less than appetising and its long-serving chef seems to be incompetent

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart has his ear pierced, while Homer is fired from the nuclear power plant and finds a new job - as the captain of a submarine in the US Navy. Featuring the guest voices of Rod Steiger and Bob Denver

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart is horrified at Marge's attempts to make him befriend Ralph Wiggum - until he learns his police chief dad has a master key to every shop in Springfield

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Interior designer Carlos Buller kicks off a series of dinner parties in Manchester and impresses his guests with his unusual flat. However, a dispute with budding artist Karen Oulton threatens his chance of winning the £1,000 prize at the end of the week

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Susan Brickell, a housing officer who has never thrown a formal dinner party before, hosts the second dinner party in Manchester. She aims to serve holiday-inspired Greek fare, but nerves get the better of her. To make matters worse, she is suffering from a hangover following the previous night's get-together

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Performance artist and vegetarian Dan Cumberland prepares three curries and a dal in the hope of winning the £1,000 prize when he hosts the third night of the competition in Manchester. Stuart Burke throws his saffron tea away while the host is not looking, but is surprisingly won over by the main course. However, problems arise when it comes to serving the kulfi dessert, which is frozen solid

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Contemporary artist Karen Oulton tries to claw her way back into her fellow contestants' good books by conjuring up an international feast for her dinner party in Manchester. However, she quickly manages to upset nightclub manager Stuart Burke with her strategy of preparing a four-course meal, when everyone else is cooking three

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Nightclub manager Stuart Burke hosts the final meal of the week in Manchester, and hopes to secure the top prize with an inspired modern British menu. The evening gets off to a promising start as the food lover serves cocktails to his guests, but takes a turn for the worse when an argument ends in tantrums and tears

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jean Johansson helps Glasgow couple Colin and Richard, who have a £200,000 budget to purchase a property in the resort of Playa del Ingles on Gran Canaria

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Danni Menzies helps a family from North Shields search for a property in the picturesque coastal town of La Duquesa on Spain's Costa del Sol

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A festive edition from Fitzpatrick Referrals, featuring some famous faces hoping that Noel Fitzpatrick can perform wonders for their much-loved animal family members. Pop star Will Young brings in his pitbull Labrador cross Diamond, which has been suffering with a painful limp, and Noel suspects cruciate ligament damage, while supermodel Yasmin Le Bon and her daughters rush in when their three-year-old French bulldog Willow collapses at home

programme.title: Four Weddings and a Funeral (1994)
programme.desc: A London man's misfortunes in love look set to change when he meets an American beauty. However, the prospect of a transatlantic relationship succeeding seems slim, so the pair go their separate ways - only for him to realise he is unable to get her out of his mind. Richard Curtis's comedy, with Hugh Grant, Andie MacDowell, John Hannah, Charlotte Coleman, Kristin Scott Thomas, Simon Callow and James Fleet

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Paul Hollywood, Prue Leith and Matt Lucas are joined by Tom Allen to celebrate Christmas in the famous white tent. Joining in the festivities are Jamie and Rosie from 2019, Ruby from 2018 and James from 2017, all back and ready to battle in the festive fight for the Christmas Star Baker title. Plus, Alexandra Burke performs her magical version of Silent Night

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: A round-up of memorable moments from the show over the course of the year, as armchair critics including Giles and Mary, Jenny and Lee, Ellie and Izzi, Mary and Marina, the Siddiquis, and Amira and Iqra give their thoughts on the year's TV output

programme.title: Back to the
programme.desc: Lenny Henry is the first of four celebrities taking viewers on a guided tour of all things Channel 4. He examines how Mary Whitehouse was decrying blasphemy in Brookside with a week of the channel's launch, and looks at the success of Countdown. Cameras also focus on cult live music show The Tube, and hit shows Whose Line Is It Anyway, and The Comic Strip Presents

programme.title: The Snowman and the Snowdog
programme.desc: Animated sequel to Raymond Briggs' classic festive tale The Snowman, telling the story of another youngster's magical Christmas. A boy's snowman and snowdog come to life at the stroke of midnight and take him on an adventure to the North Pole, where he and his new companions meet an assortment of colourful characters, including Santa himself, before returning home - where a wonderful surprise awaits

programme.title: Junior Bake Off
programme.desc: Harry Hill hosts as the remaining 10 contestants begin finals week, making caramel in the technical challenge and creating chocolate cakes in the showstopper

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Jon and Lynne Scopes from Stockport have thought about buying a property abroad for years and have viewed more than 40 apartments without success, but have at least decided their ideal area is Mijas on Spain's Costa del Sol. Jasmine Harman has the daunting task of finding them the perfect property for their £100,000 budget, but will anything meet their exacting standards?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Jasmine Harman helps house hunters find their dream homes in popular winter sun destinations. Potential buyers explore the beautiful Caribbean island of Barbados

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Ben Hillman helps Mike and Amanda Gadd find a holiday home on the Algarve, exploring five properties around their favourite town of Vilamoura for their £127,000 budget

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Retirees Peter and Glynnis Tabberer want to spend half of each year in Florida, close to Sarasota, so Ben Hillman shows them how far their £100,000 budget will stretch

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: The death of Grampa's old army friend reveals the whereabouts of priceless pictures looted during the Second World War, and Bart is asked to help recover the collection before Mr Burns gets his hands on it

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Marge takes a job as an estate agent, but her honesty with potential buyers costs her lucrative sales and gets her into hot water with the boss, who issues an ultimatum. The Flanders agree to buy one of the agency's houses - unaware of the property's gruesome past

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Fashion journalist Robert Johnston hosts the first dinner party in south London, serving Stilton ice-cream and tomato sorbet, slow-roasted pork with chilli and fennel, and Sephardic orange and almond cake

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Trainee aqua-aerobics instructor Jennifer Joseph hosts the second dinner party in south London, preparing a mountain of home-cooked Caribbean food and asking her guests to dress in hoodies and jeans. However, her plans to create a relaxed atmosphere fail when Joe Kempster's incessant chatter begins to annoy the other diners

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Bricklayer Joe Kempster hosts the third dinner party in south London. He prides himself on making all his dishes from scratch and hopes to compensate for past misunderstandings with the other guests by preparing a meal featuring a mystery Greek main course

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Hospital technician Karen Hill hosts the penultimate dinner party in south London. She hopes to impress her guests by preparing seven dishes, including Chinese duck, French pate, Greek lamb, Turkish rice and American cheesecake - but the extensive menu causes her problems

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Majid Ravandi hosts the final dinner party in south London, but fails to impress with a hot prawn starter and a dry main course. As the meal progresses, a bickering Joe and Karen threaten to spoil the cook's friendly demeanour, before the winner of the £1,000 prize is announced

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Laura Hamilton helps a Gloucestershire couple find a holiday home in Fuerteventura that is large enough to accommodate three generations of their family

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Laura Hamilton searches for a retirement home on Spain's Costa de Almeria for Ted and Diane Brewer, who met on holiday 20 years ago and would now like to live in the sun

programme.title: Snackmasters
programme.desc: Fred Sirieix pits a leading chocolatier against a virtuoso pastry chef to replicate one of the nation's best-loved festive chocolate brands - Quality Street. As ever, the chefs must work out the secret techniques and recipes behind the household favourites before creating their own versions to be judged by a panel of Quality Street insiders and experts. But with so many to choose from, will they be expected to remake the green triangle, the orange crunch or the iconic purple one?

programme.title: Planes, Trains and Automobiles (1987)
programme.desc: A stressed businessman just wants to make it home to spend Thanksgiving with his family. However, when his flight is forced to land miles from its destination, he finds himself sharing a disaster-strewn journey across America with an amiable but slobbish salesman. Comedy road movie, starring Steve Martin, John Candy, Laila Robins, Kevin Bacon and Michael McKean

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: Opinionated viewers the Siddiquis, Giles and Mary, Jenny and Lee, Ellie and Izzi, Mary and Marina, and Amira and Iqra look back on the year's biggest and best shows, including Strictly Come Dancing, Countdown, This Morning, Yorkshire Tea vs PG Tips: Battle of the Brews, Nigella's Cook, Eat, Repeat, CBeebies Bedtime Stories, The Undoing, First Dates, Borat Subsequent Moviefilm, Jamie: Keep Cooking and Carry On, Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?, Elephant, The Great British Bake Off, The Crown and Naked Attraction

programme.title: Back to the
programme.desc: The comedian examines Channel 4 in the 1990s, when its output included The Word, The Big Breakfast, Father Ted, Brass Eye and Drop the Dead Donkey, and viewers were introduced to future stars Ricky Gervais, Sacha Baron Cohen, Simon Pegg and Dermot O'Leary.

Finally, Stewart and Vic head to Lancashire to recover over £3,000 owed by a garage to a dissatisfied customer

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: The dog unit embarks on a desperate manhunt amid fears that an armed and dangerous character is roaming the streets of Lincoln, while Craggy closes in on a suspected shoplifter with a bag full of surprises. Officers from Lincolnshire's Roads Policing Unit also tackle a would-be stunt rider and a boozed-up biker who is raising hell on the A-roads

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Jon Moon and his dog Kanto are hot on the heels of some burglary suspects, while spot-checks by the unit on the A1 turn up a carrier bag full of cash. Jason Baxter rushes to the scene of an accident where the driver was thrown clear of the car, only for another crash to take place - this time involving a line of vehicles

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Barry Peach outsmarts a disqualified driver, handler Jon Peacock takes his new sniffer dog Jack to carry out a drugs warrant, Carl Warrener makes a jaw-dropping discovery when he pulls a car over in the early hours and Brian Conville gets mobbed when he deals with a reckless learner driver

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: PC Barry Peach hunts for a man wanted for assault, officers are hot on the heels of a suspected drug dealer, a drunken driver tries every trick in the book to avoid giving a breath sample and dog-handler Jon Peacock has a late-night encounter with two unusual law-breakers

programme.title: Criminals: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Journalist Nick Wallis joins police forces around the nation to discover how CCTV and technological advances are helping to bring criminals to justice. programme.title: McDonald's vs Burger King: Burger Wars
programme.desc: Documentary examining how the fast food giants grew from humble origins to become two of the most recognisable brands in the world. The programme assesses the chains' marketing ploys and promotional techniques, while vloggers and food critics compare the rivals' most notable products and the ways in which they are presented. Industry insiders also reveal the secrets behind both chains' success and how they see off the competition

programme.title: Inside Lidl at Christmas
programme.desc: With Britain facing its toughest December in memory, this programme goes behind the doors of the budget supermarket to find out how it is preparing for Christmas - and how it keeps its prices down. The Wynne family put Lidl through its paces by trying out its turkey and trimmings, there's a Christmas pudding taste test and a look at the store's decorations and random gifts

programme.title: Fifty Shades Darker (2017)
programme.desc: The bookish Anastasia Steele and damaged billionaire playboy Christian Grey continue their sado masochistic relationship, while their safety is threatened by strangers from his past. programme.title: Murder, She Wrote

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Jessica meets an old friend who happens to be one of the best homicide detectives in Los Angeles, and helps provide the vital clue in the case that ended his career. Starring Angela Lansbury and Barry Newman

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: A woman disrupts Cabot Cove's mayoral election campaign by claiming one of the candidates is her secret husband and the father of her five children. Jessica steps in to investigate when she is found dead. Ruth Roman guest stars

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit

Detective drama, starring Peter Falk, Stephen Caffrey, Gary Hershberger and Robert Culp

Drama, starring Christopher Meloni and Mariska Hargitay

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit

programme.desc: A crime-scene lab technician is murdered in a manner remarkably similar to how one of her predecessors died in 1957, and someone anonymously texts pictures of the victim to Mac and Jo. Guest starring Lee Majors (The Six Million Dollar Man) and Spencer Grammer (Greek)

Hard-hitting drama, starring Christopher Meloni and Ice-T

Drama, starring Christopher Meloni, BD Wong and Mariska Hargitay

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit

programme.title: The Enemy Within
programme.desc: When an NSA analyst is killed in a car crash, Keaton and his team realise that she has stolen a piece of cloaking software that she intends to pass on to Tal. Shepherd decodes the encrypted message that Tal had left for the analyst, which reveals exactly where it was supposed to be delivered. Seizing their opportunity, Keaton asks Shepherd to go undercover, posing as the now dead analyst. Shepherd meets with Tal's contact, a young man called Braden. programme.desc: April is covered in bumps that have disfigured her arms, and Genner has flown all the way from the Philippines for Dr Lee to help with his bumpy nose

programme.title: Dr Pimple Popper
programme.desc: Dermatological surgeon Dr. Sandra Lee is visited by Amber who has thousands of tumours on her face. Tatiana has an embarrassing bump

programme.title: Dr Pimple Popper
programme.desc: Louis is covered in dry, scaly skin, and Jennifer cannot focus on her career due to a large unicorn-like lump on her forehead

programme.title: Dr Pimple Popper
programme.desc: Amber has hundreds of tumours on her face, and sisters Cheri and Janice suffer from matching lumps. Meanwhile, Tatiana has an embarrassing bump on her rear

programme.title: Dr Pimple Popper
programme.desc: Patrick's nose is covered by huge bumps and he can barely breathe, and Matt has suffered from neurofibromatosis his entire life and is in incredible pain

programme.title: Dr Pimple Popper
programme.desc: The story of Melissa, who covered her huge lipoma with a sweatshirt for years. Tyler's fear of cancer stopped his head lumps being diagnosed, and Tahj is covered in painful boils

programme.title: Dr Pimple Popper
programme.desc: Lisa fears the bump growing out of the back of her head could be cancerous, and two teenage brothers have matching lumps

programme.title: Dr Pimple Popper
programme.desc: Ronen has a huge but mysterious lump above his knee. Meanwhile, Amber is suffering from a debilitating skin disease that no one understands

programme.title: Dr Pimple Popper
programme.desc: Anthony's cyst is treated in the area called the Triangle of Death, and Shane wants to get a lump removed off his back

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore prevents an old leather sofa, some shelves and seven rusty chairs from being dumped at the tip in Surrey, and sets about transforming them in the hope they can be sold

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Kate Bliss and Paul Laidlaw's final trip in the 1960s Volvo sees them plot a course through Kent. Paul finds himself in Dover and hears about one plucky swimmer who believed bagpipes would help him across the English Channel, while Kate infiltrates a once top secret organisation for the afternoon to learn more about their history. The duo later head for an auction in Bury St Edmunds, Suffolk

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Charles Hanson and Natasha Raskin Sharp set off in their 1969 Morris Minor from the Firth of Clyde in search of bargains, making their way through the Scottish Borders to an auction in Carlisle

programme.title: Helicopter ER
programme.desc: Paramedics carry out a critical medical procedure on an elderly motorist. The team races to the scene of a serious accident involving a cyclist

programme.title: Ambulance
programme.desc: Specialist trauma team Pete and Matt are called to the victim of a knife attack, carrying out urgent medical intervention at the scene in a bid to save the man's life. Paramedics Christine and Chris start their day shift with a man who has collapsed in Worcester town centre after a suspected drug overdose. A 96-year-old reports that an elbow wound, caused by falling off his mobility scooter earlier in the week, will not stop bleeding, so crewmates Mark and Nieaal drive 20 minutes on blue lights in the dead of night to treat him

programme.title: Ambulance Australia
programme.desc: Paramedics Mat and Patia race to help a woman who has given birth in a car. Trainee Millie shows incredible composure to get through an intense emergency call

programme.title: Ghost Adventures
programme.desc: Ghost hunters Zak Bagans, Nick Groff and Aaron Goodwin visit Kentucky, where they investigate the site of one of the bloodiest battles of the American Civil War

programme.title: Most Haunted
programme.desc: Part two of three. The team encounters aggressive poltergeist activity and disembodied footsteps as the investigation into the Leopard Inn continues

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Helicopter ER
programme.desc: The team help a woman whose leg is trapped under the wheel of a lorry. And, a man suffers spinal injuries after falling off his motorbike.

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Charles Hanson and Natasha Raskin Sharp set off in their 1969 Morris Minor from the Firth of Clyde in search of bargains, making their way through the Scottish Borders to an auction in Carlisle

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: TV chef Ainsley Harriott and presenter Anne Diamond take on the antiques-hunting challenge. Travelling in and around London on their search for auction-worthy items, Anne finds out why Britain became a nation of tea-lovers and Ainsley learns about the world's first celebrity chef

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Teleshopping Feature. Your one-stop-shop for all things cruise; bringing you the hottest destinations, ground-breaking ships and the latest deals to ensure your next cruise is your greatest one

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: The story of the volunteer lifeboat crews and lifeguards of the RNLI who rescue thousands of people and save hundreds of lives around our coastline and on the River Thames every year. In the first episode, a father and daughter find themselves treading water for almost an hour hundreds of metres out at sea, and three fishermen swim for their lives as their trawler is smashed onto rocks in Ireland. Elsewhere in London, an unlucky tourist is minutes from hypothermia after falling into the Thames

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: A kayaker goes missing for an hour off the coast of Bridlington, East Yorkshire, and a crew member on his first call-out races to a capsized dinghy to find a man losing consciousness in the water. Plus, when a fishing trawler sinks off the coast of the Shetland Islands, the fishermen and RNLI crew aboard must swim for their lives. Narrated by Steven Mackintosh

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: In Ireland, the Castletownbere crew race to rescue a lone yachtsman, who has lost his mast and is drifting 45 miles out to sea. Meanwhile, a team search for a woman in Minehead who has gone missing on a cliff top walk, and for an injured horse rider on a remote beach, the Porthcawl crew are the only way to emergency medical help

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: Crews are tasked to find two teenage paddle boarders in Bangor, Northern Ireland, and the Tenby crew in west Wales must race to a dog who's fallen over a cliff. Meanwhile, on the north Norfolk coast, the harrowing hovercraft rescue of three young sisters still brings back painful memories for the Hunstanton crew

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: In south west Wales, the Tenby lifeboat crew races to a kayaker with suspected back and neck injuries after being caught out by a freak wave. Neighbouring Cornish crews must join forces in an epic 11-hour battle to stop a 3,600-ton trading vessel breaking up on the rocks. And the Dart and Salcombe crews are astonished to be paged to the biggest shout of their lives

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: On one of the hottest days of the year, the Dover RNLI crew race to a stricken Channel swimmer enduring difficulties within eyesight of the French coast. In Mudeford, there are growing fears for a dinghy sailor four miles out in rough seas and refusing to accept help. And the Tenby team are called to a runaway yacht which is on the verge of running aground

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: When a climber falls in a remote cave on the Gower Peninsula, the Mumbles crew are called in to search and rescue. In Portreath, lifeguards battle to save a teenager who's determined to surf despite red flag warnings. And on the Isle of Wight, the Yarmouth crew must locate a man in the water amongst over a thousand boats competing in the Round the Island Race

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: In Ilfracombe, a father and son are in danger after jumping into rescue a drowning dog. The Minehead crew must scour the cliffs and shoreline when a young woman goes missing in a distressed state. And when they're paged to find a man seen entering the Thames, the Tower team face the possibility they may not find him in time

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: The Aberystwyth crew must improvise when a local paraglider crash-lands at the bottom of steep cliffs. When an adventurous seal is discovered on the steps of a block of flats, Redcar's newest recruit is first on the scene, and in Minehead, a challenging rescue forces an inexperienced crew member to consider her future

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: As the summer sun comes out, so do the funseekers, and in Southend a family of five sightseeing a mile off shore are caught out by the tide. The Shoreham crew are paged in the early hours after a drunken festival-goer jumps in the river, and the Brighton crew fear the worst when a man tombstones off the pier at low tide

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: In Stockport, Greater Manchester, Sarah Moore sets about transforming a beaten-up chest of drawers, a sack barrow and some copper pipes. Thanks to the help of furniture designer Zoe and metal worker Bex Simon, Sarah later has money to hand over for all of her finds, but just how much cash was there to be made from the Stockport trash?

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: The experts head for Derbyshire, where Charles Hanson is on his home turf, enlisting the aid of his two-year-old daughter and hitching a ride on a horse and cart before investing in a collection of medieval items dredged from a river bed. Meanwhile, Natasha Raskin Sharp visits the world's oldest lift shaft

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip

programme.title: The Queen and I
programme.desc: New republican prime minister Jack Barker strips the royals of their assets and titles and the Queen and her family are forced to move to a rundown council estate. The locals don't exactly roll out the red carpet, but new neighbours Bev, Tony, Violet and Spiggy prove to be unlikely allies. Comedy based on Sue Townsend's book, starring David Walliams, Samantha Bond, Frances Barber, Amanda Abbington, Johnny Vegas and Julia McKenzie

programme.title: Cinderella: After Ever After
programme.desc: Cinderella has just married her Prince Charming, but soon has a rude awakening after realising married life isn't all it's cracked up to be when you have an image-obsessed, floss-dancing, hip-hop rapping husband. She also fights to save the King from the scheming of her evil stepmother Madame Blackheart, and her conspiring and moronic stepsisters Dumbella and Rubella. Family comedy, starring Sian Gibson, David Walliams, Tom Courtenay and Celia Imrie

programme.title: David Attenborough's Rise of Animals
programme.desc: Part one of two. The naturalist explores the evolution of vertebrates over the past 500 million years, beginning with a look at how remarkable advances allowed primitive fish to make the radical move on to land, and then take to the skies with the advent of flight. He uses fossils of both ancient and living species combined with CGI and film to chart their journey out of the water to populate all corners of the globe

programme.title: David Attenborough's Rise of Animals
programme.desc: The naturalist concludes his two-part exploration of the evolution of vertebrates over the past 500 million years by looking at the history of mammals. He traces their journey from humble origins as tiny nocturnal forest-dwellers to their astonishing explosion in diversity and domination of the world following the death of the dinosaurs

programme.title: Attenborough's Journey
programme.desc: Documentary following David Attenborough as he journeys around the world to make his series First Life, which explores the origins of life on Earth. As he visits locations including the Great Barrier Reef, the deserts of Morocco and the glaciers of Canada, the broadcaster and wildlife expert explains his life-long fascination with the natural world, and also travels back to Leicestershire, the county in which he spent his childhood

programme.title: TV's Greatest Gameshows

programme.title: Freddie Mercury: The Tribute Concert
